X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

for authentication.

for MAC authentication. The password can be either in
ciphertext or plaintext format.

radius-server-group.description: RADIUS server group to use for authentication.

ArubaAaaMacauth_PasswordInfo.description: Group for  password to be used for authentication.

ciphertext-password.description:
The ciphertext password to be used for authentication in the
RADIUS request messages. This is applicable only when password
type is CIPHER_TEXT.

password.description:
The plaintext password to be used for authentication in the
RADIUS request messages. This is applicable only when password
type is PLAIN_TEXT.
password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

ArubaAaaMacauth_PasswordInfoGet.description: Group for  password to be used for authentication.

RADIUS request messages. This is applicable only when password

customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

ArubaAaaProfile_AccountingSystemSwitchCo.description: Group for system level accounting configurations.

x-enumDescriptions.START_STOP: Send start and stop accounting record

acct-server-group.description:
RADIUS server group to which accounting updates will be sent
when accounting-mode is START_STOP or STOP_ONLY.

interim-update-enable.description:
Enables the device to send interim network accounting updates
to the configured accounting server when accounting-mode is
START_STOP.

interim-update-interval.description:
Specifies the time interval(in minutes) between two successive
accounting updates sent to the server when interim update is
enabled.

interim-update-onreauth-enable.description:
Enables the device to send interim network accounting updates
to the configured accounting server on successful client
reauthentication when accounting-mode is START_STOP.

local-accounting.description:
Enables local accounting for port-access clients in the system
when accounting-mode is START_STOP or STOP_ONLY.

ArubaAaaProfile_AccountingSystemSwitchCo.description: Group for system level accounting configurations.

x-enumDescriptions.START_STOP: Send start and stop accounting record

acct-server-group.description:
RADIUS server group to which accounting updates will be sent
when accounting-mode is START_STOP or STOP_ONLY.

interim-update-enable.description:
Enables the device to send interim network accounting updates
to the configured accounting server when accounting-mode is
START_STOP.

interim-update-interval.description:
Specifies the time interval(in minutes) between two successive
accounting updates sent to the server when interim update is
enabled.

interim-update-onreauth-enable.description:
Enables the device to send interim network accounting updates
to the configured accounting server on successful client
reauthentication when accounting-mode is START_STOP.

local-accounting.description:
Enables local accounting for port-access clients in the system
when accounting-mode is START_STOP or STOP_ONLY.

ArubaAaaProfile_AuthenticationSystemSwit.description: Group for switch specific system level authentication configurations.

allow-vlan-tagged.description:
Configure authenticator ports to apply VLAN changes immediately and
apply tagged VLAN membership changes immediately on AAA enabled ports.

auto-vlan-enable.description:
Enable automatic creation of a VLAN for authenticated clients if the
VLAN is not already available on the device.

cache-replace-mode.description:
Replacement mode to be applied when cache limit is reached
and more clients have to be added to the cache.

x-enumDescriptions.FIFO: Specifies oldest cache entry can be replaced with new entry.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Specifies cache entry can't be overwritten till expiry.

cache-timeout.description:
The period of time in hours for which an authenticated
client's information will be cached.

enable.description:
Enables caching authenticated clients and their associated
role information. Clients are assigned to their previously
authenticated role.

persistent-storage-enable.description:
Enables writing cached client information to the device's
persistent storage unit.

persistent-storage-write-interval.description:
Interval between consecutive cached client information
writes to persistent storage file in seconds.
Flash write occurs only if there is a change in cached
client's information.

x-enumDescriptions.ENABLED:
Client can move between interfaces without authentication itself
on the new interface.
x-enumDescriptions.SECURE_ENABLED:
Client can move between interfaces but only after authenticating
itself on the new interface.

use-lldp-data.description: Authenticate clients using information from their LLDP packets.

ArubaAaaWebauth_WebauthSystemConfig.description: Group for Web authentication system-wide configuration.

radius-response.description: Use RADIUS server response as access denied message.

radius-auth-method.description: The type of RADIUS authentication method to use for authentication.

radius-server-group.description: RADIUS server group to use for authentication.

x-enumDescriptions.CACHED: Retain current session attributes during reauthentication.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Do not use backup authentication methods.

dhcp-addr.description: Configure DHCP address range for web authentication.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed
shortened format followed by a slash and CIDR mask
(0 <= mask <= 128).

dhcp-lease.description: Configure DHCP lease time for web authentication.

ewa-server-address.description: IP address or FQDN of the Ewa server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

page-path.description: Path to the authentication page on the external web authentication server.

ArubaApSystem_ApSystemSchemaGET.description:
Manage global system settings for Aruba Access Points, including time zone,
location, cluster mode, LED control, and operational parameters. These configurations
define device identity, network integration, security policies, and system-wide behaviors
such as country code, virtual controller settings, and advanced features like CPU management
and thermal controls. This profile references Alias profiles for reusable configuration templates.
Use this API to retrieve the list of AP System profiles.

profile.description:
Defines a single AP System profile containing all system-level configurations for one or more
Access Points. Each entity is identified by name. Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete
an instance.

advanced-zone.description:
Enable or disable the advanced zone feature that can configure up to 32 SSIDs.
Since the mapping method of the WLAN index and BSSID index are different,
when you change the advanced zone configuration, the BSSID is removed and created again.
When advanced zone is enabled:
 The WLAN SSID profile will remain inactive without the zone.
 The WLAN SSID profile will remain inactive without the zone.
 A zone can be assigned to a maximum of up to 16 SSIDs.
 However, if the extended SSID is disabled,
 a zone can be assigned to a maximum of up to 14 SSIDs wherein the first two virtual APs are reserved for mesh.

advertise-ap-health-ie.description:
Control AP to enable/disable 'Over the Air AP Rescue' feature. This feature is enabled by default.
'Over the Air AP Rescue' feature controls AP to broadcast the AP health IE through the wireless SSIDs.
The AP health IE contains many flags that can reflect current AP status like uplink status,
Central connectivity status. Once AP lost Central connection, this AP health IE can be broadcast through
wireless beacons so that our Aruba APs nearby can hear this beacon and send the AP health IE to Central.
This will allow the user or engineer to understand the status of the issue AP and take measures to rescue it.

advertise-location.description:
Enable advertising location info in beacon frame.
Disable by default.

allow-new-aps.description: Allows the new access points to join the AP cluster. This API is AOS8 specific.

allow-new-aps-alias.description: Alias for allow new aps. This API is AOS8 specific

items.description:
Specifies the MAC address of an AP permitted to join the cluster. Available on firmware versions up to 10.0.0.
This setting is specific to AOS8 platforms.

application-monitoring.description:
Enables application monitoring on the AP.
Available on firmware versions 8.6.0 to 8.11.99 only.

cluster-security.description:
Cluster Security DTLS configuration for IAP.
Enabling cluster-security dtls will form Datagram TLS connection between Conductor and Member APs.

allow-low-assurance-devices.description: Allow non-TPM (Trusted-Platform-Module) devices to connect over DTLS

allow-non-dtls-members.description: Do not allow non-DTLS enabled APs to connect

conductor.description: Provisions the Instant AP as a conductor Instant AP. This API is AOS8 specific.

content-filtering.description:
Content filtering configuration at system level.
This option control access to websites and applications based on their content, adding more security.

country-code.description: Defines the country of operation of an AP.

country-code-alias.description: Alias for virtual controller country code.

dhcp-option82-xml.description:
Configure DHCP Option 82 XML file. The AP inserts circuit and remote ID information
into DHCP requests from clients.

x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT_DHCPOPT82_1_XML:
Default DHCP Option 82 XML file 1. Values for Circuit and Remote ID's are as follows:
Circuit ID: AP Name, AP MAC Address, Extended AP MAC Address,
Remote ID: User's MAC Address.
x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT_DHCPOPT82_2_XML:
Default DHCP Option 82 XML file 2. Values for Circuit and Remote ID's are as follows:
Circuit ID: AP MAC Address, Extended AP MAC Address, SSID Tunnel,
Remote ID: User's MAC Address.
x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT_DHCPOPT82_3_XML:
Default DHCP Option 82 XML file 3. Values for Circuit and Remote ID's are as follows:
Circuit ID: User's MAC Address,
Remote ID: AP Name, Extended AP MAC Address.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: No DHCP Option 82 XML file will be used.

dhcp-option82-xml.x-typeDescription: Enumeration of allowed DHCP Option 82 XML file names.

dhcp-option82-xml-vlan-list.description:
VLAN or VLAN range for which DHCP Option 82 XML applies. Allowed values: a single VLAN (1-4094)
or a range (e.g., 10-100). This leaf can only be configured when dhcp-option82-xml is set to a valid XML file.
dhcp-option82-xml-vlan-list.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

disable-factory-reset.description:
Disable the factory reset function on the AP. Prevent the manual hard reset to factory default by pressing
down reset button for 5 seconds.

drt-upgrade-disable.description: Disable the automatic download and installation of DRT updates on the AP managed by Aruba Central.

dynamic-cpu-mgmt.description: Enable/Disable Dynamic CPU management to manage resources across different functions performed by an AP.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO:
Configure the AP to automatically enable or disable CPU management feature during run-time.
When configured, the AP determines the need for enabling or disabling CPU management, based
on the real-time load calculations taking into account all different functions that the CPU
needs to perform. The auto option is the default and recommended setting.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Disable the CPU management feature, typically for small networks. This setting protects the user experience.
x-enumDescriptions.ENABLE:
Enable the CPU management feature. When configured, the client and network management functions
are protected. This setting helps in large networks with a high client density.

enet-vlan.description: Configures VLAN for the upstream switch to which the AP is connected.

extended-ssid.description:
This command allows you to configure additional WLAN SSIDs.
Extended SSID is enabled in the factory default settings of APs.

firsthop-security.description:
Controls first hop security features on the AP. When enabled, security features are active.
Available on firmware versions 10.7.1 and later.
By default these security features are enabled on the AP.

ip-mode.description:
Selects the IP mode for packet processing.
IPV4_ONLY processes only IPv4 packets; IPV4_PREFER prioritizes IPv4 over IPv6.

x-enumDescriptions.IPV4_ONLY: Enables system processing of IPv4 packets.

ip-mode.x-typeDescription: IP mode to process IPv6 or IPv4 packets.

itm.description:
Enable Intelligent Thermal Management(ITM) on the AP. When enabled, certain operations of the
AP will be throttled down to reduce its internal temperature. The limitations of AP operations
is defined by the priority assigned for reduction steps configured in Intelligent Power Monitoring(IPM)
API. IPM must be enabled for ITM to function.

lacp.description:
Enable/Disable/Remove the static LACP(Link Aggregation Control Protocol) configuration.
When an AP boots up, it forms the LACP according to the static configuration.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: This parameter enables the dynamic LACP configuration. The AP will work on LACP mode only if the peer switch works on.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE:
This parameter disables the static LACP configuration. The AP will not work on LACP mode even it detects any LACP PDUs
from the peer switch.
x-enumDescriptions.ENABLE:
This parameter enables the static LACP configuration. The AP will work on LACP mode irrespective of whether or not the
peer switch works on the LACP mode.

led.description: Enable LED display on an AP.

led-override.description: Override the LED action for LED operation mode normal.

led-tap.description: Enable LED tap detection on APs. Currently, this feature is only supported on AP605H.

mas-integration.description: Enables mobility Access Switch integration on an AP.

module.description:
List of logging module configurations for specifying severity levels by type, process,
and subcategory. Capacity constraints apply on a per-context basis.

severity.description:
Minimum severity for logging.
Range: EMERGENCY, ALERT, CRITICAL, ERROR, WARNING, NOTICE, INFO, DEBUG.
severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

type-process-subcategory.description: Key combination of type, process and subcategory separated by '~'.

mtu.description:
Configure the MTU size for tunnel and br0 interfaces, and uplink interface such as 3G or 4G.
The configured MTU size is applied when the uplink changes.

out-of-service-hold-on-time.description:
Configure a hold on time in seconds, after which out-of-service operation is triggered.
For example, if the Internet is unreachable, the effect of this out-of-service state impacts the SSID
availability after the configured hold on time.

poe-power-optimization-enable.description:
Enable optimization minimizes the POE draw of the AP and may disable some parts of the AP. The USB
and Ethernet port (eth1) are shut down on the AP when this option is enabled. The AP will operate in
the full power mode when this option is disabled. It is disabled by default on the AP.

recovery-ssid-backoff-time.description:
Configures the backoff time in minutes before bringing up the recovery SSID.
When the AP disconnects from Central, the recovery SSID will be brought up after this backoff period.
The recovery SSID type can be configured in the wlan-ssid profile and provides access to the AP's troubleshooting interface.

subnet.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

subnet-mask.description: Subnet mask for the management subnet address.
subnet-mask.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

summer-time.description: Set the software clock to begin and end daylight saving time on a recurring basis.

end-day.description: The weekday when the time change end.

end-hour.description: The time, in hours and minutes, that the time change end.

end-month.description: The month when the time change end.

end-week.description: The time change end FIRST/SECOND/THIRD/FOURTH/LAST week of the month.

x-enumDescriptions.FIRST: The first week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.FOURTH: The fourth week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.LAST: The last week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.SECOND: The second week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.THIRD: The third week of the month.

end-week.x-typeDescription: Enumeration for specifying the week of the month (FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, LAST).

start-day.description: The weekday when the time change begin.

start-hour.description: The time, in hours and minutes, that the time change begin.

start-month.description: The month when the time change begin.

start-week.description: The time change begin FIRST/SECOND/THIRD/FOURTH/LAST week of the month.

x-enumDescriptions.FIRST: The first week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.FOURTH: The fourth week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.LAST: The last week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.SECOND: The second week of the month.
x-enumDescriptions.THIRD: The third week of the month.

start-week.x-typeDescription: Enumeration for specifying the week of the month (FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, LAST).

swarm-mode.description: Provision an Instant AP in the standalone, single AP, or cluster mode. This API is AOS8 specific.

mode.description:
Provisions the Instant AP in the standalone, single AP, or cluster mode.
Range: CLUSTER, STANDALONE, SINGLEAP.
Note: SINGLEAP value is unsupported on firmware versions up to 8.7.9.

x-enumDescriptions.CLUSTER: Instant AP in the cluster mode, it can form a cluster with other Virtual Controller Instant APs in the same VLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.SINGLEAP:
Instant AP in the single AP mode, it does not send or receive management frames such as mobility packets, roaming packets,
hierarchy beacons through the uplink port.
x-enumDescriptions.STANDALONE: Instant AP in the standalone mode, it cannot join a cluster of Instant APs even if there are other Instant APs in the same VLAN.

mode.x-typeDescription: Provisions the Instant AP in the standalone, single AP, or cluster mode.

sys-vlan.description:
System-VLAN for Microbranch AP. This is L3-Enabled VLAN, mapped to IPMS-system-IP-Pool.
IPMS service will use this VLAN-id, allocate a /32 IP from the corresponding system-ip-pool,
and populate the inner-ip field in ap-system profile for each Microbranch AP.

tx-blanking.description:
Controls TX blanking for Ultra Tri-Band (UTB) channel scanning. When enabled, resolves 5GHz/6GHz co-existence interference.
Available only on models: AP-735A, AP-755A.

vc-dnsip-alias.description: Alias for virtual controller dnsip. This API is AOS8 specific.

vc-vlan-alias.description: Alias for virtual controller vlan. This API is AOS8 specific.

virtual-controller-dnsip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

virtual-controller-ip.description:
Assigns an IPv4 address to the virtual controller for AP management. Available on firmware
versions up to 10.0.0. This setting is specific to AOS8 platforms.
virtual-controller-ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

virtual-controller-ipv6.description:
Assigns an IPv6 address to the virtual controller for AP management. Available on firmware
versions up to 10.0.0. This setting is specific to AOS8 platforms.
virtual-controller-ipv6.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

virtual-controller-name.description: Defines a unique name for the virtual controller.

virtual-controller-vlan.description: Configures the virtual controller settings. This API is AOS8 specific.

gateway.description: Configures a gateway for the virtual controller.
gateway.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

mask.description: Configures a subnet mask for the virtual controller.
mask.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

vlan.description: Associates a VLAN ID with the virtual controller.
vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description:
This configures a zone name for the AP. You can configure zone settings on an AP and the SSID profile, to assign an SSID to a specific AP.
To assign an SSID to a specific AP, the AP zone name must be configured on the WLAN SSID profile.

advanced-zone.description:
Enable or disable the advanced zone feature that can configure up to 32 SSIDs.
Since the mapping method of the WLAN index and BSSID index are different,
when you change the advanced zone configuration, the BSSID is removed and created again.
When advanced zone is enabled:
 The WLAN SSID profile will remain inactive without the zone.
 The WLAN SSID profile will remain inactive without the zone.
 A zone can be assigned to a maximum of up to 16 SSIDs.
 However, if the extended SSID is disabled,
 a zone can be assigned to a maximum of up to 14 SSIDs wherein the first two virtual APs are reserved for mesh.

advertise-ap-health-ie.description:
Control AP to enable/disable 'Over the Air AP Rescue' feature. This feature is enabled by default.
'Over the Air AP Rescue' feature controls AP to broadcast the AP health IE through the wireless SSIDs.
The AP health IE contains many flags that can reflect current AP status like uplink status,
Central connectivity status. Once AP lost Central connection, this AP health IE can be broadcast through
wireless beacons so that our Aruba APs nearby can hear this beacon and send the AP health IE to Central.
This will allow the user or engineer to understand the status of the issue AP and take measures to rescue it.

advertise-location.description:
Enable advertising location info in beacon frame.
Disable by default.

allow-new-aps.description: Allows the new access points to join the AP cluster. This API is AOS8 specific.

allow-new-aps-alias.description: Alias for allow new aps. This API is AOS8 specific

items.description:
Specifies the MAC address of an AP permitted to join the cluster. Available on firmware versions up to 10.0.0.
This setting is specific to AOS8 platforms.

application-monitoring.description:
Enables application monitoring on the AP.
Available on firmware versions 8.6.0 to 8.11.99 only.

cluster-security.description:
Cluster Security DTLS configuration for IAP.
Enabling cluster-security dtls will form Datagram TLS connection between Conductor and Member APs.

allow-low-assurance-devices.description: Allow non-TPM (Trusted-Platform-Module) devices to connect over DTLS

allow-non-dtls-members.description: Do not allow non-DTLS enabled APs to connect

conductor.description: Provisions the Instant AP as a conductor Instant AP. This API is AOS8 specific.

content-filtering.description:
Content filtering configuration at system level.
This option control access to websites and applications based on their content, adding more security.

country-code.description: Defines the country of operation of an AP.

country-code-alias.description: Alias for virtual controller country code.

dhcp-option82-xml.description:
Configure DHCP Option 82 XML file. The AP inserts circuit and remote ID information
into DHCP requests from clients.

x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT_DHCPOPT82_1_XML:
Default DHCP Option 82 XML file 1. Values for Circuit and Remote ID's are as follows:
Circuit ID: AP Name, AP MAC Address, Extended AP MAC Address,
Remote ID: User's MAC Address.
x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT_DHCPOPT82_2_XML:
Default DHCP Option 82 XML file 2. Values for Circuit and Remote ID's are as follows:
Circuit ID: AP MAC Address, Extended AP MAC Address, SSID Tunnel,
Remote ID: User's MAC Address.
x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT_DHCPOPT82_3_XML:
Default DHCP Option 82 XML file 3. Values for Circuit and Remote ID's are as follows:
Circuit ID: User's MAC Address,
Remote ID: AP Name, Extended AP MAC Address.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: No DHCP Option 82 XML file will be used.

dhcp-option82-xml.x-typeDescription: Enumeration of allowed DHCP Option 82 XML file names.

dhcp-option82-xml-vlan-list.description:
VLAN or VLAN range for which DHCP Option 82 XML applies. Allowed values: a single VLAN (1-4094)
or a range (e.g., 10-100). This leaf can only be configured when dhcp-option82-xml is set to a valid XML file.
dhcp-option82-xml-vlan-list.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

disable-factory-reset.description:
Disable the factory reset function on the AP. Prevent the manual hard reset to factory default by pressing
down reset button for 5 seconds.

drt-upgrade-disable.description: Disable the automatic download and installation of DRT updates on the AP managed by Aruba Central.

dynamic-cpu-mgmt.description: Enable/Disable Dynamic CPU management to manage resources across different functions performed by an AP.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO:
Configure the AP to automatically enable or disable CPU management feature during run-time.
When configured, the AP determines the need for enabling or disabling CPU management, based
on the real-time load calculations taking into account all different functions that the CPU
needs to perform. The auto option is the default and recommended setting.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Disable the CPU management feature, typically for small networks. This setting protects the user experience.
x-enumDescriptions.ENABLE:
Enable the CPU management feature. When configured, the client and network management functions
are protected. This setting helps in large networks with a high client density.

enet-vlan.description: Configures VLAN for the upstream switch to which the AP is connected.

extended-ssid.description:
This command allows you to configure additional WLAN SSIDs.
Extended SSID is enabled in the factory default settings of APs.

firsthop-security.description:
Controls first hop security features on the AP. When enabled, security features are active.
Available on firmware versions 10.7.1 and later.
By default these security features are enabled on the AP.

ip-mode.description:
Selects the IP mode for packet processing.
IPV4_ONLY processes only IPv4 packets; IPV4_PREFER prioritizes IPv4 over IPv6.

x-enumDescriptions.IPV4_ONLY: Enables system processing of IPv4 packets.

ip-mode.x-typeDescription: IP mode to process IPv6 or IPv4 packets.

itm.description:
Enable Intelligent Thermal Management(ITM) on the AP. When enabled, certain operations of the
AP will be throttled down to reduce its internal temperature. The limitations of AP operations
is defined by the priority assigned for reduction steps configured in Intelligent Power Monitoring(IPM)
API. IPM must be enabled for ITM to function.

lacp.description:
Enable/Disable/Remove the static LACP(Link Aggregation Control Protocol) configuration.
When an AP boots up, it forms the LACP according to the static configuration.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: This parameter enables the dynamic LACP configuration. The AP will work on LACP mode only if the peer switch works on.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE:
This parameter disables the static LACP configuration. The AP will not work on LACP mode even it detects any LACP PDUs
from the peer switch.
x-enumDescriptions.ENABLE:
This parameter enables the static LACP configuration. The AP will work on LACP mode irrespective of whether or not the
peer switch works on the LACP mode.

led.description: Enable LED display on an AP.

led-override.description: Override the LED action for LED operation mode normal.

led-tap.description: Enable LED tap detection on APs. Currently, this feature is only supported on AP605H.

mas-integration.description: Enables mobility Access Switch integration on an AP.

module.description:
List of logging module configurations for specifying severity levels by type, process,
and subcategory. Capacity constraints apply on a per-context basis.

severity.description:
Minimum severity for logging.
Range: EMERGENCY, ALERT, CRITICAL, ERROR, WARNING, NOTICE, INFO, DEBUG.
severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

type-process-subcategory.description: Key combination of type, process and subcategory separated by '~'.

mtu.description:
Configure the MTU size for tunnel and br0 interfaces, and uplink interface such as 3G or 4G.
The configured MTU size is applied when the uplink changes.

out-of-service-hold-on-time.description:
Configure a hold on time in seconds, after which out-of-service operation is triggered.
For example, if the Internet is unreachable, the effect of this out-of-service state impacts the SSID
availability after the configured hold on time.

poe-power-optimization-enable.description:
Enable optimization minimizes the POE draw of the AP and may disable some parts of the AP. The USB
and Ethernet port (eth1) are shut down on the AP when this option is enabled. The AP will operate in
the full power mode when this option is disabled. It is disabled by default on the AP.

recovery-ssid-backoff-time.description:
Configures the backoff time in minutes before bringing up the recovery SSID.
When the AP disconnects from Central, the recovery SSID will be brought up after this backoff period.
The recovery SSID type can be configured in the wlan-ssid profile and provides access to the AP's troubleshooting interface.

subnet.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

subnet-mask.description: Subnet mask for the management subnet address.
subnet-mask.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

x-enumDescriptions.THIRD: The third week of the month.

mode.description:
Provisions the Instant AP in the standalone, single AP, or cluster mode.
Range: CLUSTER, STANDALONE, SINGLEAP.
Note: SINGLEAP value is unsupported on firmware versions up to 8.7.9.

x-enumDescriptions.CLUSTER: Instant AP in the cluster mode, it can form a cluster with other Virtual Controller Instant APs in the same VLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.SINGLEAP:

hierarchy beacons through the uplink port.

virtual-controller-name.description: Defines a unique name for the virtual controller.

virtual-controller-vlan.description: Configures the virtual controller settings. This API is AOS8 specific.

gateway.description: Configures a gateway for the virtual controller.
gateway.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

mask.description: Configures a subnet mask for the virtual controller.
mask.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

vlan.description: Associates a VLAN ID with the virtual controller.
vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description:
This configures a zone name for the AP. You can configure zone settings on an AP and the SSID profile, to assign an SSID to a specific AP.
To assign an SSID to a specific AP, the AP zone name must be configured on the WLAN SSID profile.

ArubaApSystem_AuthenticationSystemApConf.description: Group for AP specific system level authentication configurations.

auth-survivability-cachetime.description:
This parameter specifies the lifetime in hours for the cached access credential in the local Survival Server. When the specified cache-lifetime expires, the cached access credential is deleted from the managed device.
The default value is 24h, the time range is 1-168 for AP.

denylist-time.description: Sets the duration in seconds for denylisting clients due to an ACL rule trigger.

gateway-key-caching.description: Controls gateway key caching for fast roaming. When enabled, keys are cached to reduce authentication latency.

local-keyfetch.description:
Configure AP local network key fetch for fast roaming. This feature enables APs to fetch PMK keys
locally from the Home AP using L2 broadcast messages as an alternative to KMS on-demand key fetch.
This helps reduce roaming latency and improves client connectivity when the AP-Central connection
is down or KMS latency is high.

x-enumDescriptions.ENCKEY_DEFAULT:
Enable AP local key fetch with default encryption keys provided by KMS. This allows APs to
fetch PMK keys from the Home AP using L2 broadcast messages when Central connection is down
or KMS latency is high.
x-enumDescriptions.KEYFETCH_OFF:
Disable AP local key fetch feature. When disabled, APs will not participate in local network
L2 broadcast-based PMK key fetch requests or responses .

local-keyfetch.x-typeDescription: AP local network key fetch mode for fast roaming.

max-pmk-cache-threshold.description: AP support the max pmk cache count.

onboard-event-connection-record.description: Controls whether onboarding event connection records are maintained. Available on firmware versions 10.6.0 and later.

preference-of-san-over-cn.description:
Prioritizes Subject Alternative Name (SAN) over Common Name (CN) when validating Captive Portal certificates.
If SAN contains a legal domain, it is used as the Captive Portal domain. Available on firmware versions 10.6.0 and later.

radius-termination-action.description: Enables RADIUS Termination-Action attribute processing. Available from firmware versions 10.4.0 and 8.10.0.

rfc-3576-server-udp-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

ArubaCountermon_CountermonSchemaGET.description:
The counter monitor is an ASIC counter monitoring mechanism designed
to be less resource intensive which periodically collects a set of
port error counters and updates the latest values into OVSDB.

profile.description: Counter monitor configuration profile.

poll-interval.description: System resource data polling interval in seconds.
System resource data polling interval in seconds.
System resource data polling interval in seconds.

column-names.description:
Name of the columns from the database table to monitor. This
value can be empty. If this value is left empty, the rule will
not be activated and an error will be generated.

column-name.description: Specify the column name to monitor.

filter-condition.description:
Condition to filter the operation results. This value can be empty.
Filter conditions are not mandatory, if they are not added, no filters
will be applied to the rule.

column-name.description: Specify the column to filter by.

filter-value.description:
Value that must be present in the column or referenced column
for the condition to evaluate to 'true'

referenced-column.description:
Column from the referenced table used to evaluate the value
given for the condition.

last-n-limit.description: Number of changes to be stored in the monitor type 'LAST_N'.

name.description: Name used to identify each rule.

operations.description:
Operation types to be monitored. This value can be empty. If
this value is left empty, the rule will not be activated and
an error will be generated.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: All operation types

empty, the rule will not be activated and an error will be
generated.

Make a count of the number of operations going to the
database.

Register all the operations going to the database in a
buffer. The content can be dumped in a file later.
x-enumDescriptions.LAST_N:
Keep track of the last N transactions going to the
database. Unlike 'JOURNAL', this just keeps a finite,
well-known number of transactions.

ArubaDdns_DdnsSchemaGET.description:
Dynamic DNS (DDNS) using IETF RFC 2136 protocol automatically updates
DNS records when device IP addresses change through DHCP or uplink
modifications. DDNS uses TSIG (Transaction Signature) authentication
for secure zone updates to authoritative DNS servers. Configure DDNS
update intervals, server lists with authentication keys (Gateway),
and PTR record options (Access Point). Server IPs are IPv4 only. Use
this API to retrieve the list of DDNS profiles.

profile.description:
DDNS profile defining IETF RFC 2136 update configuration including
server addresses, TSIG authentication keys, update intervals, and
PTR record settings. Each entity is identified by profile name. Use
these APIs to create, modify, or delete a DDNS profile instance.

ArubaDdns_DynamicDnsIetf.description:
Configure Dynamic DNS (DDNS) updates using the IETF method.
Use this to refresh DNS records when device IPs change. AP supports
interval and enable/PTR toggles; Gateway supports a server list with
TSIG and the update interval. Server IPs are IPv4 only.

enable-ptr.description: Enables Dynamic DNS updates with PTR records.

interval.description: Periodic refresh interval for DDNS updates (900–86400 seconds, default 900).

server.description:
Gateway DDNS servers used to publish uplink and DHCP client IP changes.
Servers use IPv4 and authenticate with a TSIG key.

ip.description: DDNS server IP address. Only IPv4 version is supported

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

key.description:
TSIG key as algo-name:keyname:keystring.
Algorithms: hmac-sha1, hmac-sha256, hmac-md5.
Required on Gateway.

ArubaDdns_DynamicDnsIetfGet.description:
Configure Dynamic DNS (DDNS) updates using the IETF method.
Use this to refresh DNS records when device IPs change. AP supports
interval and enable/PTR toggles; Gateway supports a server list with
TSIG and the update interval. Server IPs are IPv4 only.

enable-ptr.description: Enables Dynamic DNS updates with PTR records.

interval.description: Periodic refresh interval for DDNS updates (900–86400 seconds, default 900).

server.description:
Gateway DDNS servers used to publish uplink and DHCP client IP changes.
Servers use IPv4 and authenticate with a TSIG key.

ip.description: DDNS server IP address. Only IPv4 version is supported

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

key.description:
TSIG key as algo-name:keyname:keystring.
Algorithms: hmac-sha1, hmac-sha256, hmac-md5.
Required on Gateway.

The DNS queries for the configured domains will be redirected to the domain specific DNS servers.

ArubaDns_SplitDnsTop.description: Grouping to configure Split DNS functionality with enterprise domains for IAP-VPN use cases.

split-dns.description:
When the enterprise domain is configured, the DNS resolution for domain names
in the enterprise domain list is forwarded to the default Corporate DNS server of the client.
The DNS resolution for all other domains is forwarded to the local DNS server of the AP's
uplink learned DNS server.
For example, if the enterprise domain is configured for arubanetworks.com, the DNS resolution
for host names in the arubanetworks.com domain is forwarded to the default DNS server of the client.
The DNS resolution for domain names in all other domains is forwarded to the local DNS server of the IAP.
This Split DNS functionality is supported for IAP-VPN scenarios only.
AOS8x IAP-VPN will use the enterprise domains list to split-dns, However AOS10x uses redirect
list with server ip address and domains for DNS resolutions same as BGW.

items.description:
Enterprise domain name or If an asterisk is configured in the enterprise domain list
instead of a domain name, then all DNS requests are forwarded to the
default corporate DNS server of the client.

ArubaDns_SplitDnsTopGet.description: Grouping to configure Split DNS functionality with enterprise domains for IAP-VPN use cases.

split-dns.description:
When the enterprise domain is configured, the DNS resolution for domain names
in the enterprise domain list is forwarded to the default Corporate DNS server of the client.
The DNS resolution for all other domains is forwarded to the local DNS server of the AP's
uplink learned DNS server.
For example, if the enterprise domain is configured for arubanetworks.com, the DNS resolution
for host names in the arubanetworks.com domain is forwarded to the default DNS server of the client.
The DNS resolution for domain names in all other domains is forwarded to the local DNS server of the IAP.
This Split DNS functionality is supported for IAP-VPN scenarios only.
AOS8x IAP-VPN will use the enterprise domains list to split-dns, However AOS10x uses redirect
list with server ip address and domains for DNS resolutions same as BGW.

items.description:
Enterprise domain name or If an asterisk is configured in the enterprise domain list
instead of a domain name, then all DNS requests are forwarded to the
default corporate DNS server of the client.

ArubaDumpServer_DumpServerSchemaGET.description:
Configure TFTP and SCP dump server for storing core dump files. Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) is
a simple way for exchanging files in clear text without authentication. SCP (Secure Copy Protocol) uses
Secure Shell (SSH) mechanisms for data transfer and authentication to ensure the confidentiality of the
data in transit.. This feature is only applicable for AP.

ArubaGwSystem_GwSystemSchemaGET.description:
The Gateway System profile defines device-level operational parameters including timezone configuration, LCD menu controls, system IP addressing (controller-ip), global firewall policies, AAA authentication timers, RADIUS/TACACS+ client settings, IPv6 proxy router advertisements, DNS query intervals, and SNMP location attributes.
This profile relies on VLAN Interface and Loopback Interface configurations for network-facing parameters.
Certain features like datapath energy efficiency, LCD menu, and GPS support are hardware-dependent and available only on specific Gateway models.
Use this API to retrieve the list of Gateway System profiles.

profile.description:
A Gateway System profile instance encapsulates all device-level operational settings for a gateway.
Each profile is uniquely identified by the profile name.
Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete a Gateway System profile.

aaa-timers.description: Configures timeout intervals for AAA (Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting) operations including server failure detection, user idle sessions, logon attempts, and RADIUS accounting updates.

dead-time.description:
Dead time interval in minutes for a down authentication server.
Value of 0 disables dead time.

idle-timeout.description: Inactivity timeout for authenticated users in minutes. If no traffic is detected from a user for this duration, the session is terminated and re-authentication is required. This parameter is mutually exclusive with idle-timeout-seconds.

idle-timeout-seconds.description:
User idle timeout in seconds.
Valid range is 30-15300 seconds in multiples of 30 seconds.
This field can be configured only if idle-timeout is not configured.

logon-lifetime.description: Maximum session duration for captive portal or web-authenticated users in minutes. After this time expires, the user must re-authenticate via the captive portal even if the session is active. Value of 0 disables the maximum lifetime, allowing sessions to persist until idle timeout or explicit logout.

stats-timeout.description: Interval for sending RADIUS Interim-Update accounting messages in minutes. These updates provide periodic usage statistics (bytes transferred, session duration) to the RADIUS server for active user sessions. Mutually exclusive with stats-timeout-seconds.

stats-timeout-seconds.description: User Interim stats timeout value in seconds. This field can be configured only if stats-timeout is not configured.

contact.description: Device contact information, often an email.

cpdns-disable.description: Disables the gateway's automatic DNS redirection for captive portal detection. When enabled, the gateway will not intercept DNS queries from unauthenticated clients to force captive portal discovery. Mutually exclusive with cpdns-ip.

cpdns-ip.description: Specifies a custom IP address returned in DNS responses to unauthenticated clients, forcing redirection to the captive portal. This overrides the default behavior of returning the gateway's own IP address. Mutually exclusive with cpdns-disable. Corresponds to the `ip cp-redirect-address` CLI command.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

datapath-energy-efficiency.description:
Minimizes idle CPU spinning. It is applicable only on x86 based platforms.
Not supported on Gateway models: 7005, 7008, 7010, 7024, 7030, 7205, 7210, 7220, 7240, 7240XM, 7280.

dns-query-interval.description: Interval for re-resolving DNS hostnames configured in RADIUS and authentication server profiles. This ensures the gateway maintains current IP addresses for servers specified by FQDN rather than static IP.

fips.description: Enable the Fips knob on the Aruba Gateway.

firewall.description: Defines device-wide firewall behaviors that apply to all traffic regardless of user role or VLAN. These settings control inter-user traffic isolation, jumbo frame support, and VoIP QoS trust models.

deny-inter-user-bridging.description:
Prevents the forwarding of Layer2 traffic between wired or wireless users.
You can configure user role policies that prevent Layer3 traffic between users or networks but this does not block Layer2 traffic.
This option can be used to prevent traffic, such as Appletalk or IPX from being forwarded.
If enabled, traffic (all nonIP traffic) to untrusted port or tunnel is also blocked.

deny-inter-user-bridging-allowed-address.description: Configure IPv4/IPv6 addresses to be exempted when deny-inter-user-bridging is enabled. Total IPv4 and IPv6 allowed addresses can't exceed 256

ip-address.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address that needs to be allowed

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted decimal notation.
Excludes invalid/reserved addresses:
- 0.0.0.0 (unspecified address)
- 127.0.0.0/8 (loopback addresses)
- 224.0.0.0/4 (multicast addresses, 224-239.x.x.x)
- 255.255.255.255 (limited broadcast address)

An IPv6 address represented in standard notation (full, compressed, or mixed formats).
Does not include zone identifiers (e.g., %interface).
Excludes invalid/reserved addresses:
- :: (unspecified address, all zeros)
- ::1 (loopback address)
- ff00::/8 (multicast addresses, ffxx:...)

mac-address.description: mac-address that needs to be allowed

deny-inter-user-traffic.description:
Denies downstream traffic between users in a wireless network (untrusted users) by disallowing layer2 and layer3 traffic.
This parameter does not depend on the deny-inter-userbridging parameter being enabled or disabled.

drop-ipv4-options.description: When enabled, all IPv4 packets with options are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

jumbo.description: Enable Jumbo frames processing with default MTU value 9216

jumbo-mtu.description: Enable Jumbo frames processing with custom MTU value. Valid range is 1789-9216

voip-qos-trusted.description: Prioritizes the RTP traffic based on the DSCP value set by the end user device

drop-ipv6-options.description: When enabled, all IPv6 packets with options are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

ipv4-configuration.description:
System IPv4 configurations, allows you to set either
the loopback interface or a specific VLAN interface
address. If the system IPv4 is not configured, then
the first configured VLAN interface address is selected
by the device.

device-interface-type.description:
Specifies the interface type used for the system controller IPv4 address.
When set to VLAN, the device-vlan or device-vlan-alias parameter must also be specified to identify which VLAN interface to use. Corresponds to the `controller-ip` CLI command.

x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK: Uses the loopback interface IP address as the system identifier. Recommended for stable addressing as loopback interfaces remain up regardless of physical link state.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Uses a VLAN interface IP address as the system identifier. The system will use the primary IP address configured on the specified VLAN.

device-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the interface type used for system-level IP addressing (controller-ip). This determines which IP address the gateway uses to identify itself to managed devices, RADIUS servers, and routing protocols.

device-vlan.description: References an existing VLAN Interface profile by ID. The primary IPv4 address configured on this VLAN will be used as the controller-ip. Valid only when device-interface-type is set to VLAN.
device-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

device-vlan-alias.description:
Alias for device VLAN identifier, only applicable when device type is set to VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with device VLAN identifier field.

ipv6-certification-mode.description: Enables IPv6 certification test mode for compliance validation. This mode adjusts protocol timers and behaviors to match IPv6 conformance test requirements (e.g., IPv6 Ready Logo testing). Should only be enabled in test environments.

ipv6-configuration.description:
System IPv6 configurations, allows you to set either
the loopback interface or a specific VLAN interface
address. If the system IPv6 is not configured, then
the first configured VLAN interface address is selected
by the device.

device-address.description:
Specific IPv6 address to use as the controller-ipv6 identifier. This address must be pre-configured on the VLAN interface specified in device-vlan.
Valid only when device-interface-type is set to VLAN. This parameter is restricted to device-level scope and cannot be applied at site or global levels.
It cannot co-exist with ipv6-device-vlan-alias.
device-address.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

device-interface-type.description: Device interface type to indicate either loopback or VLAN interface.

x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK: Uses the loopback interface IP address as the system identifier. Recommended for stable addressing as loopback interfaces remain up regardless of physical link state.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Uses a VLAN interface IP address as the system identifier. The system will use the primary IP address configured on the specified VLAN.

device-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the interface type used for system-level IP addressing (controller-ip). This determines which IP address the gateway uses to identify itself to managed devices, RADIUS servers, and routing protocols.

device-vlan.description:
Device VLAN identifier, only applicable when device type is set to VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with ipv6-device-vlan-alias.
device-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ipv6-device-vlan-alias.description:
Alias for IPv6 device VLAN and address configuration, only applicable when device interface type is VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with direct device-vlan and device-address.

ipv6-enable.description: Master enable for IPv6 protocol support on the gateway. When disabled, the device will drop all IPv6 packets and will not participate in IPv6 routing or forwarding.

ipv6-proxy-ra.description: Configures the gateway to act as an IPv6 router advertisement proxy, forwarding RA messages between network segments. This allows IPv6 clients on downstream VLANs to receive router advertisements from upstream routers without direct Layer 2 connectivity.

advertise-interval.description: Interval for sending unsolicited IPv6 Router Advertisement messages in seconds. Per RFC 4861, this value must be between 180 and 1800 seconds.

ipv6-transition-mode.description:
Enable IPv6 transition-mode.
Supported modes: NAT64.

x-enumDescriptions.NAT64: Network Address Translation from IPv6 to IPv4 (NAT64) as defined in RFC 6146. Enables IPv6-only clients to access IPv4-only services through stateful address translation.

ipv6-transition-mode.x-typeDescription: IPv6 transition mechanism for facilitating communication between IPv6 and IPv4 networks. NAT64 is currently the only supported mode.

lcd.description:
Controls visibility of maintenance operations in the physical LCD menu panel on the front of the gateway.
Available only on Gateway models with LCD displays: 7010, 7030, 7024, 7205, 7210, 7220, 7240, 7240XM, 7280, 9240.
Restricting menu options prevents unauthorized physical access to factory reset, system halt, and image upgrade functions.

main-factory-default.description:
Controls visibility of the factory-default option in the LCD maintenance menu. When enabled (true), the option is visible and accessible.
When disabled (false), factory reset cannot be initiated from the physical LCD panel, providing protection against accidental or unauthorized device resets.

main-upgrade-image-part.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

menu.description:
Master enable/disable for all LCD menu functionality. When disabled, the LCD screen becomes read-only and displays only system status information.
All sub-menu controls (maintenance, factory-default, etc.) are automatically disabled.

upgrade-image-partition0.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

upgrade-image-partition1.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

location.description: Free-form text describing the physical location of the device. This value is exposed via SNMP sysLocation OID (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.6). Maximum length is platform-dependent.

logging.description: Controls local log message verbosity by module type, process, and severity level. This affects what diagnostic information is captured in the device's internal log buffers and forwarded to external syslog servers.

module.description: Defines per-module logging rules combining type, process, severity, and optional filtering by process, subcategory, or trace identifier. Each rule controls the minimum subcategory level for messages from specific system components.

x-enumDescriptions.APPRF: Logging for APPRF feature on Service Controllers
x-enumDescriptions.BLE_DAEMON_S: Logging for BLE Daemon Process on 90xx controllers
x-enumDescriptions.IP_FLOW_EXPORT: Logging for IP Flow Export Process
x-enumDescriptions.PACKETFILTER: Logging for Packet filtering of messaging and control frames
x-enumDescriptions.WMS: Logging for Wireless Management (Master switch only)

trace.description: MAC address or identifier for targeted debugging. Valid only when logging type is set to arm-user-debug, peer-debug, or user-debug. Filters log messages to only those associated with the specified client or peer device.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IEEE 802 MAC address

x-enumDescriptions.FACILITY: Facility to use when logging to Remote Syslog Server

type-process-subcategory.description:
key for module. This key is the combination of type, process and subcategory separated by ~
type        - Logging type.
process     - Logging process.
subcategory - Logging subcategory.

loopback-interface.description: References an existing Loopback Interface profile by ID. Used in conjunction with ipv4-configuration and ipv6-configuration to specify the system controller-ip address source.

radius.description: Configures the gateway's behavior as a RADIUS client including the NAS-IP-Address sent in RADIUS packets, the source interface for RADIUS communication, and the UDP port for receiving RFC 3576 (Dynamic Authorization) messages from RADIUS servers.

nas-ip.description: Radius client NAS IP address. This field can be configured only if nas-vlan is not configured.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

nas-vlan.description: References an existing VLAN Interface profile by ID. The primary IP address configured on this VLAN will be used as the RADIUS client NAS-IP-Address attribute. Mutually exclusive with nas-ip.
nas-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

port.description: This field configures UDP port to receive RFC 3576 server requests.

src-interface-loopback.description:
IP address of loopback interface will be used as source address in Radius packets.
This field can be configured only if radius src-interface-vlan is not configured.

src-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN ID. IP address from this vlan interface will be used as source address in Radius packets.
This field can be configured only if radius src-interface-loopback is not configured.
src-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tacacs.description: Configures the gateway's behavior as a TACACS+ client, specifically controlling the source interface used for TACACS+ authentication and authorization requests to TACACS+ servers.

src-interface-loopback.description:
IP address of loopback interface will be used as source address in TACACS packets.
This field can be configured only if tacacs src-interface-vlan is not configured.

src-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN ID. IP address from this vlan interface will be used as source address in TACACS packets.
This field can be configured only if tacacs src-interface-loopback is not configured.
src-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

timezone.description: Configures the device's timezone offset from UTC for accurate timestamp representation in logs, SNMP traps, and the LCD display. The combined hour and minute offsets determine the local time zone.

tunnel-loop-prevention.description: Prevents Layer 2 forwarding loops that can occur when traffic is encapsulated between multiple tunnels terminating on the same gateway. When enabled, the gateway tracks tunnel packet origins and drops packets that would create circular forwarding paths between GRE, IPsec, or L2TPv3 tunnels.

vpn-session-acl.description: Security policy applied to VPNC Overlay traffic.

aaa-timers.description: Configures timeout intervals for AAA (Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting) operations including server failure detection, user idle sessions, logon attempts, and RADIUS accounting updates.

dead-time.description:
Dead time interval in minutes for a down authentication server.
Value of 0 disables dead time.

idle-timeout.description: Inactivity timeout for authenticated users in minutes. If no traffic is detected from a user for this duration, the session is terminated and re-authentication is required. This parameter is mutually exclusive with idle-timeout-seconds.

idle-timeout-seconds.description:
User idle timeout in seconds.
Valid range is 30-15300 seconds in multiples of 30 seconds.
This field can be configured only if idle-timeout is not configured.

logon-lifetime.description: Maximum session duration for captive portal or web-authenticated users in minutes. After this time expires, the user must re-authenticate via the captive portal even if the session is active. Value of 0 disables the maximum lifetime, allowing sessions to persist until idle timeout or explicit logout.

stats-timeout.description: Interval for sending RADIUS Interim-Update accounting messages in minutes. These updates provide periodic usage statistics (bytes transferred, session duration) to the RADIUS server for active user sessions. Mutually exclusive with stats-timeout-seconds.

stats-timeout-seconds.description: User Interim stats timeout value in seconds. This field can be configured only if stats-timeout is not configured.

contact.description: Device contact information, often an email.

cpdns-disable.description: Disables the gateway's automatic DNS redirection for captive portal detection. When enabled, the gateway will not intercept DNS queries from unauthenticated clients to force captive portal discovery. Mutually exclusive with cpdns-ip.

cpdns-ip.description: Specifies a custom IP address returned in DNS responses to unauthenticated clients, forcing redirection to the captive portal. This overrides the default behavior of returning the gateway's own IP address. Mutually exclusive with cpdns-disable. Corresponds to the `ip cp-redirect-address` CLI command.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

datapath-energy-efficiency.description:
Minimizes idle CPU spinning. It is applicable only on x86 based platforms.
Not supported on Gateway models: 7005, 7008, 7010, 7024, 7030, 7205, 7210, 7220, 7240, 7240XM, 7280.

dns-query-interval.description: Interval for re-resolving DNS hostnames configured in RADIUS and authentication server profiles. This ensures the gateway maintains current IP addresses for servers specified by FQDN rather than static IP.

fips.description: Enable the Fips knob on the Aruba Gateway.

firewall.description: Defines device-wide firewall behaviors that apply to all traffic regardless of user role or VLAN. These settings control inter-user traffic isolation, jumbo frame support, and VoIP QoS trust models.

deny-inter-user-bridging.description:
Prevents the forwarding of Layer2 traffic between wired or wireless users.
You can configure user role policies that prevent Layer3 traffic between users or networks but this does not block Layer2 traffic.
This option can be used to prevent traffic, such as Appletalk or IPX from being forwarded.
If enabled, traffic (all nonIP traffic) to untrusted port or tunnel is also blocked.

deny-inter-user-bridging-allowed-address.description: Configure IPv4/IPv6 addresses to be exempted when deny-inter-user-bridging is enabled. Total IPv4 and IPv6 allowed addresses can't exceed 256

ip-address.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address that needs to be allowed

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted decimal notation.
Excludes invalid/reserved addresses:
- 0.0.0.0 (unspecified address)
- 127.0.0.0/8 (loopback addresses)
- 224.0.0.0/4 (multicast addresses, 224-239.x.x.x)
- 255.255.255.255 (limited broadcast address)

An IPv6 address represented in standard notation (full, compressed, or mixed formats).
Does not include zone identifiers (e.g., %interface).
Excludes invalid/reserved addresses:
- :: (unspecified address, all zeros)
- ::1 (loopback address)
- ff00::/8 (multicast addresses, ffxx:...)

mac-address.description: mac-address that needs to be allowed

deny-inter-user-traffic.description:
Denies downstream traffic between users in a wireless network (untrusted users) by disallowing layer2 and layer3 traffic.
This parameter does not depend on the deny-inter-userbridging parameter being enabled or disabled.

drop-ipv4-options.description: When enabled, all IPv4 packets with options are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

jumbo.description: Enable Jumbo frames processing with default MTU value 9216

jumbo-mtu.description: Enable Jumbo frames processing with custom MTU value. Valid range is 1789-9216

voip-qos-trusted.description: Prioritizes the RTP traffic based on the DSCP value set by the end user device

drop-ipv6-options.description: When enabled, all IPv6 packets with options are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

ipv4-configuration.description:
System IPv4 configurations, allows you to set either
the loopback interface or a specific VLAN interface
address. If the system IPv4 is not configured, then
the first configured VLAN interface address is selected
by the device.

device-interface-type.description:
Specifies the interface type used for the system controller IPv4 address.
When set to VLAN, the device-vlan or device-vlan-alias parameter must also be specified to identify which VLAN interface to use. Corresponds to the `controller-ip` CLI command.

x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK: Uses the loopback interface IP address as the system identifier. Recommended for stable addressing as loopback interfaces remain up regardless of physical link state.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Uses a VLAN interface IP address as the system identifier. The system will use the primary IP address configured on the specified VLAN.

device-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the interface type used for system-level IP addressing (controller-ip). This determines which IP address the gateway uses to identify itself to managed devices, RADIUS servers, and routing protocols.

device-vlan.description: References an existing VLAN Interface profile by ID. The primary IPv4 address configured on this VLAN will be used as the controller-ip. Valid only when device-interface-type is set to VLAN.
device-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

device-vlan-alias.description:
Alias for device VLAN identifier, only applicable when device type is set to VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with device VLAN identifier field.

ipv6-certification-mode.description: Enables IPv6 certification test mode for compliance validation. This mode adjusts protocol timers and behaviors to match IPv6 conformance test requirements (e.g., IPv6 Ready Logo testing). Should only be enabled in test environments.

ipv6-configuration.description:
System IPv6 configurations, allows you to set either
the loopback interface or a specific VLAN interface
address. If the system IPv6 is not configured, then
the first configured VLAN interface address is selected
by the device.

device-address.description:
Specific IPv6 address to use as the controller-ipv6 identifier. This address must be pre-configured on the VLAN interface specified in device-vlan.
Valid only when device-interface-type is set to VLAN. This parameter is restricted to device-level scope and cannot be applied at site or global levels.
It cannot co-exist with ipv6-device-vlan-alias.
device-address.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

device-interface-type.description: Device interface type to indicate either loopback or VLAN interface.

x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK: Uses the loopback interface IP address as the system identifier. Recommended for stable addressing as loopback interfaces remain up regardless of physical link state.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Uses a VLAN interface IP address as the system identifier. The system will use the primary IP address configured on the specified VLAN.

device-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the interface type used for system-level IP addressing (controller-ip). This determines which IP address the gateway uses to identify itself to managed devices, RADIUS servers, and routing protocols.

device-vlan.description:
Device VLAN identifier, only applicable when device type is set to VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with ipv6-device-vlan-alias.
device-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ipv6-device-vlan-alias.description:
Alias for IPv6 device VLAN and address configuration, only applicable when device interface type is VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with direct device-vlan and device-address.

ipv6-enable.description: Master enable for IPv6 protocol support on the gateway. When disabled, the device will drop all IPv6 packets and will not participate in IPv6 routing or forwarding.

ipv6-proxy-ra.description: Configures the gateway to act as an IPv6 router advertisement proxy, forwarding RA messages between network segments. This allows IPv6 clients on downstream VLANs to receive router advertisements from upstream routers without direct Layer 2 connectivity.

advertise-interval.description: Interval for sending unsolicited IPv6 Router Advertisement messages in seconds. Per RFC 4861, this value must be between 180 and 1800 seconds.

ipv6-transition-mode.description:
Enable IPv6 transition-mode.
Supported modes: NAT64.

x-enumDescriptions.NAT64: Network Address Translation from IPv6 to IPv4 (NAT64) as defined in RFC 6146. Enables IPv6-only clients to access IPv4-only services through stateful address translation.

ipv6-transition-mode.x-typeDescription: IPv6 transition mechanism for facilitating communication between IPv6 and IPv4 networks. NAT64 is currently the only supported mode.

lcd.description:
Controls visibility of maintenance operations in the physical LCD menu panel on the front of the gateway.
Available only on Gateway models with LCD displays: 7010, 7030, 7024, 7205, 7210, 7220, 7240, 7240XM, 7280, 9240.
Restricting menu options prevents unauthorized physical access to factory reset, system halt, and image upgrade functions.

main-factory-default.description:
Controls visibility of the factory-default option in the LCD maintenance menu. When enabled (true), the option is visible and accessible.
When disabled (false), factory reset cannot be initiated from the physical LCD panel, providing protection against accidental or unauthorized device resets.

main-upgrade-image-part.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

menu.description:
Master enable/disable for all LCD menu functionality. When disabled, the LCD screen becomes read-only and displays only system status information.
All sub-menu controls (maintenance, factory-default, etc.) are automatically disabled.

upgrade-image-partition0.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

upgrade-image-partition1.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

location.description: Free-form text describing the physical location of the device. This value is exposed via SNMP sysLocation OID (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.6). Maximum length is platform-dependent.

logging.description: Controls local log message verbosity by module type, process, and severity level. This affects what diagnostic information is captured in the device's internal log buffers and forwarded to external syslog servers.

module.description: Defines per-module logging rules combining type, process, severity, and optional filtering by process, subcategory, or trace identifier. Each rule controls the minimum subcategory level for messages from specific system components.
