You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

jumbo.description: Enable Jumbo frames processing with default MTU value 9216

jumbo-mtu.description: Enable Jumbo frames processing with custom MTU value. Valid range is 1789-9216

voip-qos-trusted.description: Prioritizes the RTP traffic based on the DSCP value set by the end user device

drop-ipv6-options.description: When enabled, all IPv6 packets with options are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

ipv4-configuration.description:
System IPv4 configurations, allows you to set either
the loopback interface or a specific VLAN interface
address. If the system IPv4 is not configured, then
the first configured VLAN interface address is selected
by the device.

device-interface-type.description:
Specifies the interface type used for the system controller IPv4 address.
When set to VLAN, the device-vlan or device-vlan-alias parameter must also be specified to identify which VLAN interface to use. Corresponds to the `controller-ip` CLI command.

x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK: Uses the loopback interface IP address as the system identifier. Recommended for stable addressing as loopback interfaces remain up regardless of physical link state.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Uses a VLAN interface IP address as the system identifier. The system will use the primary IP address configured on the specified VLAN.

device-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the interface type used for system-level IP addressing (controller-ip). This determines which IP address the gateway uses to identify itself to managed devices, RADIUS servers, and routing protocols.

device-vlan.description: References an existing VLAN Interface profile by ID. The primary IPv4 address configured on this VLAN will be used as the controller-ip. Valid only when device-interface-type is set to VLAN.
device-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

device-vlan-alias.description:
Alias for device VLAN identifier, only applicable when device type is set to VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with device VLAN identifier field.

ipv6-certification-mode.description: Enables IPv6 certification test mode for compliance validation. This mode adjusts protocol timers and behaviors to match IPv6 conformance test requirements (e.g., IPv6 Ready Logo testing). Should only be enabled in test environments.

ipv6-configuration.description:
System IPv6 configurations, allows you to set either
the loopback interface or a specific VLAN interface
address. If the system IPv6 is not configured, then
the first configured VLAN interface address is selected
by the device.

device-address.description:
Specific IPv6 address to use as the controller-ipv6 identifier. This address must be pre-configured on the VLAN interface specified in device-vlan.
Valid only when device-interface-type is set to VLAN. This parameter is restricted to device-level scope and cannot be applied at site or global levels.
It cannot co-exist with ipv6-device-vlan-alias.
device-address.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

device-interface-type.description: Device interface type to indicate either loopback or VLAN interface.

x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK: Uses the loopback interface IP address as the system identifier. Recommended for stable addressing as loopback interfaces remain up regardless of physical link state.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Uses a VLAN interface IP address as the system identifier. The system will use the primary IP address configured on the specified VLAN.

device-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the interface type used for system-level IP addressing (controller-ip). This determines which IP address the gateway uses to identify itself to managed devices, RADIUS servers, and routing protocols.

device-vlan.description:
Device VLAN identifier, only applicable when device type is set to VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with ipv6-device-vlan-alias.
device-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ipv6-device-vlan-alias.description:
Alias for IPv6 device VLAN and address configuration, only applicable when device interface type is VLAN.
It cannot co-exist with direct device-vlan and device-address.

ipv6-enable.description: Master enable for IPv6 protocol support on the gateway. When disabled, the device will drop all IPv6 packets and will not participate in IPv6 routing or forwarding.

ipv6-proxy-ra.description: Configures the gateway to act as an IPv6 router advertisement proxy, forwarding RA messages between network segments. This allows IPv6 clients on downstream VLANs to receive router advertisements from upstream routers without direct Layer 2 connectivity.

advertise-interval.description: Interval for sending unsolicited IPv6 Router Advertisement messages in seconds. Per RFC 4861, this value must be between 180 and 1800 seconds.

ipv6-transition-mode.description:
Enable IPv6 transition-mode.
Supported modes: NAT64.

x-enumDescriptions.NAT64: Network Address Translation from IPv6 to IPv4 (NAT64) as defined in RFC 6146. Enables IPv6-only clients to access IPv4-only services through stateful address translation.

ipv6-transition-mode.x-typeDescription: IPv6 transition mechanism for facilitating communication between IPv6 and IPv4 networks. NAT64 is currently the only supported mode.

lcd.description:
Controls visibility of maintenance operations in the physical LCD menu panel on the front of the gateway.
Available only on Gateway models with LCD displays: 7010, 7030, 7024, 7205, 7210, 7220, 7240, 7240XM, 7280, 9240.
Restricting menu options prevents unauthorized physical access to factory reset, system halt, and image upgrade functions.

main-factory-default.description:
Controls visibility of the factory-default option in the LCD maintenance menu. When enabled (true), the option is visible and accessible.
When disabled (false), factory reset cannot be initiated from the physical LCD panel, providing protection against accidental or unauthorized device resets.

main-upgrade-image-part.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

menu.description:
Master enable/disable for all LCD menu functionality. When disabled, the LCD screen becomes read-only and displays only system status information.
All sub-menu controls (maintenance, factory-default, etc.) are automatically disabled.

upgrade-image-partition0.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

upgrade-image-partition1.description: Disable image upgrade partition via LCD.

location.description: Free-form text describing the physical location of the device. This value is exposed via SNMP sysLocation OID (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.6). Maximum length is platform-dependent.

logging.description: Controls local log message verbosity by module type, process, and severity level. This affects what diagnostic information is captured in the device's internal log buffers and forwarded to external syslog servers.

module.description: Defines per-module logging rules combining type, process, severity, and optional filtering by process, subcategory, or trace identifier. Each rule controls the minimum subcategory level for messages from specific system components.

x-enumDescriptions.APPRF: Logging for APPRF feature on Service Controllers
x-enumDescriptions.BLE_DAEMON_S: Logging for BLE Daemon Process on 90xx controllers
x-enumDescriptions.IP_FLOW_EXPORT: Logging for IP Flow Export Process
x-enumDescriptions.PACKETFILTER: Logging for Packet filtering of messaging and control frames
x-enumDescriptions.WMS: Logging for Wireless Management (Master switch only)

x-enumDescriptions.WMS: Logging for Wireless Management (Master switch only)

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Logging user Process type.

severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

subcategory.description: Name of the debug subcategory type.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: Enable logging of all the sub-categories

oneOf.x-typeDescription: ARM User sub-category type.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: Enable logging of all the sub-categories

x-enumDescriptions.GP: Palo Alto Networks Global Protect logging
x-enumDescriptions.HTTP: Logging for Health Check Manager Http probe
x-enumDescriptions.UPLINK: Logging for Policy Manager Uplink task

oneOf.x-typeDescription: System sub-category type.

trace.description: MAC address or identifier for targeted debugging. Valid only when logging type is set to arm-user-debug, peer-debug, or user-debug. Filters log messages to only those associated with the specified client or peer device.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IEEE 802 MAC address

x-enumDescriptions.FACILITY: Facility to use when logging to Remote Syslog Server

type-process-subcategory.description:
key for module. This key is the combination of type, process and subcategory separated by ~
type        - Logging type.
process     - Logging process.
subcategory - Logging subcategory.

loopback-interface.description: References an existing Loopback Interface profile by ID. Used in conjunction with ipv4-configuration and ipv6-configuration to specify the system controller-ip address source.

radius.description: Configures the gateway's behavior as a RADIUS client including the NAS-IP-Address sent in RADIUS packets, the source interface for RADIUS communication, and the UDP port for receiving RFC 3576 (Dynamic Authorization) messages from RADIUS servers.

nas-ip.description: Radius client NAS IP address. This field can be configured only if nas-vlan is not configured.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

nas-vlan.description: References an existing VLAN Interface profile by ID. The primary IP address configured on this VLAN will be used as the RADIUS client NAS-IP-Address attribute. Mutually exclusive with nas-ip.
nas-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

port.description: This field configures UDP port to receive RFC 3576 server requests.

src-interface-loopback.description:
IP address of loopback interface will be used as source address in Radius packets.
This field can be configured only if radius src-interface-vlan is not configured.

src-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN ID. IP address from this vlan interface will be used as source address in Radius packets.
This field can be configured only if radius src-interface-loopback is not configured.
src-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tacacs.description: Configures the gateway's behavior as a TACACS+ client, specifically controlling the source interface used for TACACS+ authentication and authorization requests to TACACS+ servers.

src-interface-loopback.description:
IP address of loopback interface will be used as source address in TACACS packets.
This field can be configured only if tacacs src-interface-vlan is not configured.

src-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN ID. IP address from this vlan interface will be used as source address in TACACS packets.
This field can be configured only if tacacs src-interface-loopback is not configured.
src-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

timezone.description: Configures the device's timezone offset from UTC for accurate timestamp representation in logs, SNMP traps, and the LCD display. The combined hour and minute offsets determine the local time zone.

tunnel-loop-prevention.description: Prevents Layer 2 forwarding loops that can occur when traffic is encapsulated between multiple tunnels terminating on the same gateway. When enabled, the gateway tracks tunnel packet origins and drops packets that would create circular forwarding paths between GRE, IPsec, or L2TPv3 tunnels.

vpn-session-acl.description: Security policy applied to VPNC Overlay traffic.

ArubaHardwareModuleProfile_HardwareModul.description:
Configure and manage hardware module profiles.
A hardware module profile allows certain device-specific configuration to be applied
to a standalone switch, a chassis line-module or a VSF stack member. It is a mechanism
intended to ease the process of configuring these devices with the same set of
configurations. Instead of manually configuring each switch, line-module or VSF
member one-by-one, the hardware module profile collects the common configuration
settings and allows them to be applied to a specific device or a collection of
devices.
To apply a hardware module profile to a VSF member or group of VSF members, the
hw-profile name here must be referenced from their respective 'hw-profile' endpoints
in the 'switch-stack' API.
See `aruba-switch-stack:stacks/stack/members/hw-profile`.
Applicable to CX 6200, 6300, 6300L switches.
To apply a hardware module profile to a specific line-module or group of line-modules,
the profile name here must be referenced from their respective 'hw-profile' endpoint
in the switch-chassis' API.
See `aruba-switch-chassis:switch-chassis/chassis/line-modules/hw-profile`
Applicable to CX 6400, 8400X switches.
To apply a hardware module profile to a standalone switch, including devices that
support VSF but are not configured in a stack, no reference from another API is
required.

always-on-poe.description:
Always-on PoE provides ability to continue power delivery during
a soft/warm reboot. On standalone devices this applies globally,
on VSF it applies to the 'Member' and in chassis devices this is
applicable to the 'Line Cards'.

description.description: A module description, used to identify what is configured and how it is configured.

interface-group-speed-profile.description:
Some switch models have a restriction that all transceivers installed in
a group of ports must have compatible speeds. All mismatched interfaces
in the group will be disabled.

group-id.description: Device dependent interface group identifier for a set of port.

speed.description: Interface group speed. Device dependent, the possible speeds are 10g, 25g, 40g, 50g, 100g or 200g.

items.description:
Specifies the list of member or slot IDs to which this profile applies.
- For standalone switches: This list is ignored.
- For VSF stacks: List the member IDs that should use this profile.
- For chassis systems: List the slot IDs that should use this profile.
Note: For slot names like '1/3', only the slot number (e.g., 3) is used as the slot ID.

name.description: The name of the profile for the hardware module.

quick-poe.description:
Enable quick-poe configuration. quick-poe allows the PSE
to power up connected PDs soon after a cold reboot. On standalone
devices this applies globally, on VSF it applies to the 'Member' and
in chassis devices this is applicable to the 'Line Cards'.

always-on-poe.description:
Always-on PoE provides ability to continue power delivery during
a soft/warm reboot. On standalone devices this applies globally,
on VSF it applies to the 'Member' and in chassis devices this is
applicable to the 'Line Cards'.

ArubaHttpProxy_HttpProxyServersSchemaGET.description:
Use this to configure HTTP proxy settings. On AP, HTTP Proxy settings is for downloading the image from the
cloud server, or to route the web classification queries through the proxy server. On SW_CX and SW_PVOS, the
HTTP Proxy configuration is utilized to connect to Activate server and HPE ANW Central.
This feature is applicable for AP, SW_CX and SW_PVOS.

profile.description: HTTP Proxy Server profile.

address.description: HTTP Proxy server's IP address or domain name.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

items.description: Configures the proxy exception server's IP address or the domain name.

zone.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical

domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

vrf.description: Proxy server's VRF configutation.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

address.description: HTTP Proxy server's IP address or domain name.

prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

items.description: Configures the proxy exception server's IP address or the domain name.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

Configure Intelligent Power Monitoring (IPM) reduction steps and priorities. The reduction function with the highest priority is applied when the power

ArubaJobScheduler_JobSchedulerSchemaGET.description:
The job scheduler feature facilitates us to schedule a job to be
executed in the near future.  Currently, only non-interactive enable
and configuration commands are allowed to be configured as a job.
Schedules can be configured to be triggered periodically or at a specific
calendar date and time. They are categorized into periodic schedules,
calendar schedules, and one-shot schedules. Periodic schedules are based
on the fixed time interval between the two triggers.

Calendar schedules are based on the specified days of the week and days
of the month. Calendar schedules are therefore aware of the notion of
months, days, weekdays, hours, and minutes.

One-shot schedules are similar to the calendar schedule but they will
automatically disable themselves after one trigger.

This feature is useful to schedule a toggling port, check the system
health status, clear the statistics or some clean-up activities,
QoS policy changes, save the configurations, and other similar jobs.

While updating trigger-type other parameter must set to null, which are
not applicable.

cli-command.description: Command to execute when this job runs.

config-save.description: Save the running configuration to flash after the job completes.

count.description:
Maximum number of times the schedule can trigger.
If not set, no limit is applied on the number of triggers.
This is not applicable for 'one-shot' schedule type.
Once schedule is triggered count number of times, the status
changes to expired.

date-time.x-typeDescription:
A date and time, expressed in the format described in RFC3339.
That is to say:

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ+-hh:mm

where YYYY is the year, MM is the month expressed as a two-digit
month (zero padding if required), DD is the day of the month,
expressed as a two digit value. T is the literal character 'T',
HH is the hour of the day expressed as a two digit number, using
the 24-hour clock, MM is the minute of the hour expressed as a
two digit number. Z is the literal character 'Z', followed by a
timezone offset expressed in hours (hh) and minutes (mm), both
expressed as two digit numbers. The time offset is specified as
a positive or negative offset to UTC using the '+' or '-'
character preceding the offset.

Optionally, fractional seconds can be expressed after the minute
of the hour as a decimal number of unspecified precision
reflecting fractions of a second.

trigger-at.description:
Trigger at given time.
trigger-at is valid when trigger-type is TRIGGER_AT.

x-enumDescriptions.TRIGGER_AT: Fixed date and time for the schedule. This is oneshot schedule.
x-enumDescriptions.TRIGGER_EVENT: Event at which the job is triggered.
x-enumDescriptions.TRIGGER_EVERY: Periodic interval for schedule with custom time interval.

trigger-at-event.description: Runs the job when the event occur.

trigger-every.description:
Trigger every peoriodic interval. trigger-every is valid when
trigger-type is TRIGGER_EVERY.

ArubaLocalManagement_LocalManagementSche.description:
Configure local management settings of network devices via SSH, Telnet, console, or UI.
This ensures secure, controlled, and monitored access to the devices. Manage AAA
(Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting) for device local management, and it ensures
that only authorized users can access the device, perform specific actions, and that all
activities are logged for auditing purposes. This feature is applicable for all devices.

profile.description:
List of Local Management Profiles.
For CX device, only 1 profile supported.

audit-period.description: Configure audit period. Value of 0 implies disabling of audit-info.

banner-exec.description:
Banner to be displayed after a successful login with the management
interface.

text.description: For PVOS devices, the authentication banner-exec text length ranges from 1 to 3055 characters for firmware versions 16.11.0021 and earlier, and from 1 to 3068 characters for firmware versions 16.11.0022 and later.

banner-message.description: Banner to be displayed at the login prompt when a user connects to the management interface.

enforce-accept.description: WebUI users must accept the banner to login.

text.description: For PVOS devices, the authentication banner-message text length ranges from 1 to 3055 characters for firmware versions 16.11.0021 and earlier, and from 1 to 3068 characters for firmware versions 16.11.0022 and later.

bluetooth.description: Enables management access to a switch through a USB Bluetooth adapter.

baud-rate.description: Console serial port data transmission speed.

enable.description: Enables console access through the serial port.

events.description: Set level of the events displayed in the device's Events Log.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: All log events.
x-enumDescriptions.NOT_INFO: Only Critical, Warn, and Debug level log events.

flow-control.description: Console serial port flow control method.

x-enumDescriptions.NONE: No serial port flow control method used.
x-enumDescriptions.XON_XOFF: XON or XOFF used as serial port flow control method.

flow-control.x-typeDescription: Console serial port flow control method.

local-terminal.description: Enable the type of terminal for the current console sessions.

x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Raw mode with no terminal escape sequences.

local-terminal.x-typeDescription: Enables type of terminal for the current console.

max-sessions.description: Maximum concurrent sessions supported by the device.

screen-refresh.description: Configure the refresh time in seconds for menu status and counters.

serviceos-password-prompt.description: Enables the ServiceOS boot loader password prompt.

session-timeout.description: AP console session idle timeout in minutes. Applies to serial console access only and range is 0 (disable timeout) or 5-1440 minutes.

terminal.description: Enable the type of terminal for the all console sessions.

x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Raw mode with no terminal escape sequences.

terminal.x-typeDescription: Enables type of terminal for the current console.

usb-idle-timeout.description: Serial or USB console session timeout.

items.description:
Disable specific cipher authentication for SSH.
Range: AES_CBC, AES_CTR.

x-enumDescriptions.AES_CBC: Disable AES-CBC authentication. AES-CTR will be enabled.
x-enumDescriptions.AES_CTR: Disable AES-CTR authentication. AES-CBC will be enabled.

disable-dsa.description: Disable DSA for SSH and only allow RSA.

disable-kex.description: Disable key exchange (KEX) algorithm for SSH.

items.description: Disable specific MAC authentication for SSH.

hash-mgmt-password.description: Enable/Disable hashing of the management user password.

enable.description: Enable hashing of the management user password.

min-password-length.description:
Minimum password length.
If this value exceeds the current password length, the user will be prompted to change their password at the next login.

localauth.description: Enable Local authentication even if radius authentication times out.

lockout-delay.description:
Time period for which the unauthorizes users will be denylisted.
The value is measured in seconds.
For PVOS, valid range is 0..3600 seconds.

num-attempts.description:
Number of invalid login attempts before a user is blocked out of the system.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 1 to 10.
If no value is set, the default is no limit on login attempts.

user-lockout.description: User lockout configuration for management interfaces.

denylist-period.description:
Time period for which the unauthorizes users will be denylisted.
The value is measured in seconds.
For a CX device, the range is 1..3600 seconds. This value has no effect unless threshold is set.
For AP, valid range is 10..65535.

session.description: User sessions for which the lockout is applied

x-enumDescriptions.LOGIN:
Remote CLI login for SSH and Telnet.
For a CX device, this includes remote REST login also.

threshold.description:
Number of invalid login attempts before a user is blocked out of the system.
For a CX device, the range is 1..10.
If no value is set, the default is no limit on login attempts.

login-session-timeout.description:
If there is no activity for this duration the Management(Telnet, SSH and Console) session is terminated.
For a CX device, the default value is 30 minutes. A value of 0 disables the timeout.
For AP, valid range is 0 and 5-60 minitues, 0 to disable.
For GW value of '0' disables it. default 15 minutes. Range is <1-60> minutes

managed-mode-profile.description: Enable automatic configuration of the APs in the management mode.

automatic.description:
Enables the automatic mode to automatically generate the user
credentials based on AP MAC address.

x-enumDescriptions.FTP: FTP used for downloading configuration files.
x-enumDescriptions.FTPS: FTPS used for downloading configuration files.

password.description: Password credentials set by the user to enable automatic configuration when automatic is false.
password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

retry-poll-period.description:
Time interval in minutes between two retries, after which
APs can retry downloading the configuration file.

server-name.description:
Name of the server or the IP address of the server from which the
configuration file must be downloaded.

sync-day.description:
Configuration day, for example to configure Sunday as day, specify 01.
To configure the synchronization period as everyday, enter 00.

sync-hour.description: Configuration hour in the range of 0-23.

sync-minute.description: Configuration minute in the range of 0-59.

sync-window.description:
Defines a window for synchronization of the configuration file.
The default value is 3 hours.

username.description: Username credentials set by user to enable automatic configuration when automatic is false.

max-sessions-per-user.description:
Maximum concurrent sessions per user.
For a CX device, the range allowed is 1-5 sessions.

mgmt-auth-public-key.description: Management user's SSH Authentication method by public-key auth.

mgmt-auth-uname-pwd.description: Management user's SSH Authentication method by username & password.

cli-authorization.description: Configure NAE agent ActionCLI commands to require authorization.

name.description: Local Management Profile name to be configured.

adjacent-char-type-count.description:
Maximum number of adjacent characters allowed from character set.
For a Gateway device, the range of values is 0-10 max consecutive characters. A value of 0 indicates that enforcing of maximum number of consecutive character repeat is disabled by default.
For a CX device, the default value is 0 character. A value of 0 indicates that the enforcement on the adjacent characters from the character set in the password is disabled by default.

all-repeat-check.description: Enables all possible repeat checks avialable.

certificate-lock-out.description: Configuring the number of failed attempts within 3 minute window to lockout the user. Provides ability to reduce the number of login attempts that can be made in a short time. Automatically clears the lockout after configured 'lock-out' minutes. A value of 0 indicates that lockout of users is disabled.

certificate-lock-out-time.description: Configuring the number of minutes the user is locked out. The lockout is cleared without administrator intervention.

history-count.description:
Maximum number of password history count for each user.
For a CX device, the default password history count is 5 and the range is 1-5.
For a PVOS device, the default password history count is 8, with a valid range of 2 to 15.

lowercase-count.description:
Minimum number of lowercase count in a password.
For a Gateway device, the range of values is  0-10 minimum lower case characters. A value of 0 indicates that the minimum number of lower case required is disabled by default.
For a CX device, the default value is 1 character.
For a CX device, the range is 0..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 2 to 15 lowercase characters, with a default of 2.

min-char-difference.description:
Minimum number of character that the new password differes from old password.
For a CX device, the default value is 8 characters.
For a CX device, the range is 1..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier, 1..64 otherwise.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 8 to 64.

minimum-length.description:
Minimum password length required.
For a Gateway device, the default value is 6 characters and the range of values is 6-128 characters.
For a CX device, the default minimum length of the password is 8 characters.
For a CX device, the range is 1..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier, 1..64 otherwise.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 0 to 64.

numeric-count.description:
Minimum number of numeric count in a password.
For a Gateway device, the range of values is 0-10 numeric digits and the default value is 0.
For a CX device, the default value is 1 character.
For a CX device, the range is 0..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 2 to 15 numeric digits, with a default of 2.

password-aging-period.description: Password aging period for a system.

password-alert-before-expiry.description: Number of days before password aging during which user is warned of pending password expiry.

password-expired-user-login-attempts.description: Number of subsequent logins allowed after password expiry.

password-expiry-user-login-attempts-peri.description: Duration in days in which the particular logins are allowed.

password-lock-out.description: Configuring the number of failed attempts within 3 minute window to lockout the user. Provides ability to reduce the number of passwords that can be guessed in a short time. Automatically clears the lockout after configured 'lock-out' minutes. A value of 0 indicates that lockout of users is disabled.

password-lock-out-time.description: Configuring the number of minutes the user is locked out. The lockout is cleared without administrator intervention.

password-update-interval.description: Wait duration in hours before the password update.

repeat-char-check.description: Password repeat character check ensures password does not contain three of the same characters used consecutively.

repeat-password-check.description: Password repeat check ensures same password is not allowed.

repeat-username-check.description: Password repeat username check ensures same username or reverse of username is not set as password.

special-char-count.description:
Minimum number of special character count in a password.
For a Gateway device, the special characters are !, @, #, $, %, ^, &, *, <, >, {, }, [, ], :, ., comma, |, +, ~, `. The range of values is 0-10 special characters. A value of 0 indicates that the minimum number of special character required is disabled by default. The following ( ')', '(' ;, -, space, =, /, ?) are dis-allowed.
For a CX device, the default value is 1 character.
For a CX device, the range is 0..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 2 to 15 special characters, with a default of 2.

uppercase-count.description:
Minimum number of uppercase count in a password.
For a Gateway device, the range of values is 0-10 minimum upper case characters. A value of 0 indicates that minimum number of upper case required is disabled by default.
For a CX device, the default value is 1 character.
For a CX device, the range is 0..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 2 to 15 uppercase characters, with a default of 2.

password-alert-before-expiry.description: Number of days before password aging during which user is warned of pending password expiry.

password-expired-user-login-attempts.description: Number of subsequent logins allowed after password expiry.

password-expiry-user-login-attempts-peri.description: Duration in days in which the particular logins are allowed.

password-lock-out.description: Configuring the number of failed attempts within 3 minute window to lockout the user. Provides ability to reduce the number of passwords that can be guessed in a short time. Automatically clears the lockout after configured 'lock-out' minutes. A value of 0 indicates that lockout of users is disabled.

password-lock-out-time.description: Configuring the number of minutes the user is locked out. The lockout is cleared without administrator intervention.

password-update-interval.description: Wait duration in hours before the password update.

repeat-char-check.description: Password repeat character check ensures password does not contain three of the same characters used consecutively.

repeat-password-check.description: Password repeat check ensures same password is not allowed.

repeat-username-check.description: Password repeat username check ensures same username or reverse of username is not set as password.

special-char-count.description:
Minimum number of special character count in a password.
For a Gateway device, the special characters are !, @, #, $, %, ^, &, *, <, >, {, }, [, ], :, ., comma, |, +, ~, `. The range of values is 0-10 special characters. A value of 0 indicates that the minimum number of special character required is disabled by default. The following ( ')', '(' ;, -, space, =, /, ?) are dis-allowed.
For a CX device, the default value is 1 character.
For a CX device, the range is 0..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 2 to 15 special characters, with a default of 2.

uppercase-count.description:
Minimum number of uppercase count in a password.
For a Gateway device, the range of values is 0-10 minimum upper case characters. A value of 0 indicates that minimum number of upper case required is disabled by default.
For a CX device, the default value is 1 character.
For a CX device, the range is 0..32 for firmware versions 10.12 and earlier.
For PVOS device, valid values range from 2 to 15 uppercase characters, with a default of 2.

telnet-server.description: Enables Telnet access to the CLI

enable.description: Enable Telnet access to the system.

telnet-server-listen-interface.description: The type of interface that the telnet server is available.

items.description: VRF on which the telnet server is enabled/disabled.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

enable.description: Enable Telnet access to the system.
Enable Telnet access to the system.

tftp-listen-interface.description: The type of interface that the TFTP server is available.

tracking-range.description:
The maximum time period, in days, for which a user's login sessions will be tracked.
For a CX device, the default value is 30 days and the range allowed is 1..30 days.

enable.description: Enable/Disable reading data from barometer sensor.
Enable/Disable reading data from barometer sensor.

gps.description:
Configure parameters for GPS (Global Positioning System) function.
Available on firmware versions 8.10.0 to 10.0.0, and 10.4.0 and later.

dynamic-model.description:
Dynamic model of GPS, in which it includes options: AIRBORNEV1/AIRBORNEV2/AIRBORNEV4
/AUTOMOTIVE/BIKE/PEDESTRIAN/PORTABLE/SEA/STATIONARY/WRIST.
STATIONARY is default.

x-enumDescriptions.AIRBORNEV1:
GPS receiver is used by an airborne object with an acceleration of 1g (gravitational acceleration),
and that object is accelerating at a rate equal to the acceleration due to gravity on Earth.
x-enumDescriptions.AIRBORNEV2:
GPS receiver is used by an airborne object with an acceleration of 2g (gravitational acceleration),
and that object is accelerating at a rate equal to the acceleration due to gravity on Earth.
x-enumDescriptions.AIRBORNEV4:
GPS receiver is used by an airborne object with an acceleration of 2g (gravitational acceleration),
and that object is accelerating at a rate equal to the acceleration due to gravity on Earth.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTOMOTIVE: GSP receiver is used on an automobile.
x-enumDescriptions.BIKE: GPS receiver works in a bike scenario.
x-enumDescriptions.PEDESTRIAN: GPS receiver is used by a pedestrian.
x-enumDescriptions.PORTABLE: GPS receiver is used on a portable device.
x-enumDescriptions.SEA: GPS receiver is used on the sea.
x-enumDescriptions.WRIST: GPS receiver is wore on someone's wrist.

dynamic-model.x-typeDescription:
Dynamic model refers to the navigation dynamic platform model that GPS receiver uses.
It includes airborne, automative, bike, pedestrian, portable devices, sea scenario,
stationary and wearing devices.

ellipse-report-interval.description:
Interval for ellipse report when FCO response is received.
Range is from 60 to 1440 in minutes, 360 by default.

height.description:
Height of device over ground, which supports 16 fractions at most.
In meters.

indoor-sp-location.description:
An enhancement for Standard Power(SP) APs to obtain GPS ellipse data. When enabled,
APs are able to broadcast their GPS ellipse data in wireless packets, then neighbor APs with
poor GPS signal can use the received infomation to compute their own ellipses.

max-relay-distance.description:
A limit for accumulated distance from the AP that broadcasts original ellipse data.
If the distance is over this limit, AP will not choose this ellipse.

max-samples.description:
Maximum number of samples for GPS ellipse report, which is from 30 to 600, 100 by default.
This value means the maxmum samples saved on APs.

min-samples.description:
Minimum number of GPS samples for the fisrt report, its value is from 1 to 100, 30 by default.
A step for the next report till the max number. For example, first reporting happens
when there are 30 samples, the nex report happens when 60 (30 + 30) samples.

retry.description:
Whether AP sends GPS reports when number of samples reaches the maximum, but no reply
from FCO (Automatic Frequency Coordination) service. This service applies channel list
and power level to enable AP 6G radio within the USA.
Disabled by default.

sampling-interval.description:
Interval to read GPS data from chip, an integer that from 1 to 3600, in seconds.
Deault value is 10 second.
Available on firmware versions 10.7.0 and later.

state.description: Configure GPS function with options AUTO/ENABLE/DISABLE, AUTO is the default config.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: Set GPS function in auto mode.

state.x-typeDescription:
Control GPS working state.
There are three states: enable, auto and disable.

lci.description: Location Coordinate Information (LCI) configuration on AP.

height.description: Height over the specific floor, which is from -92048 to 92048.

move.description: Trend of moving, which includes NO_MOVE, EXPECT_TO_MOVE and UNKNOWN.

x-enumDescriptions.EXPECT_TO_MOVE: An object is going to move.
x-enumDescriptions.UNKNOWN: Not sure whether an object is going to move or not.

number.description: Floor number, which is from -9512 to 9512.

uncertainty.description: Uncertainty in floor management, it assigns the precision.

location.description: Location information, which includes latitude, longitude and altitude.

altitude.description: AP altitude, which support 16 fractions at most, in unit of decimal degrees.

altitude-type.description: Altitude type, which means how to disply altitude, in meters, in floors or unknown.

x-enumDescriptions.UNKNOWN: It means an unknow measurement method.

altitude-type.x-typeDescription:
Different measurement methods for altitude in Location Coordinate Information (LCI).
It includes unknow, by meters and by floors.

datum.description:
Real world coordinate systems used.
1, World Geodetic System 1984 [WGS84].
2, Combination of the North American Datum 1983 (NAD83) for
   horizontal (Latitude and Longitude) values, plus the North
   American Vertical Datum of 1988 (NAVD88) vertical datum.
3, Combination of the North American Datum 1983 (NAD83) for
   horizontal (Latitude and Longitude) values, plus the Mean
   Lower Low Water (MLLW) vertical datum.

x-enumDescriptions.NAD83_MLLW:
Combination of the North American Datum 1983 (NAD83) for horizontal (Latitude
and Longitude) values, plus the Mean Lower Low Water (MLLW) vertical datum.
x-enumDescriptions.NAD83_NAVD88_3:
Combination of the North American Datum 1983 (NAD83) for
horizontal (Latitude and Longitude) values, plus the North
American Vertical Datum of 1988 (NAVD88) vertical datum.
x-enumDescriptions.WGS88_2: Use World Geodetic System 1984 [WGS84] coordinate reference system.

latitude.description: AP latitude, which support 16 fractions at most, in unit of decimal degrees.

longitude.description: AP longitude, which support 16 fractions at most, in unit of decimal degrees.

uncertainty.description: LCI uncertainty parameters for latitude, longitude and altitude.

ArubaLogging_Logger.description:
Generated log messages may be directed to various destinations including
console, files, remote syslog servers to send messages to the log

accounting-format-native.description:
Enable sending audit log messages to the syslog server in Linux format.
If it is not enabled, then it sends in ArubaOS-CX defined log format.

enable.description: Display the live logs on console.

filter-regex.description:
POSIX regular expression filter associated
with console-logging.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: All event and debug logs.

log-type.x-typeDescription: Specifies the type of log notification.

min-severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

event-trap.description: This command enables/disables the notification of the events to be sent as traps.

facility.description: Syslog facility to be used for remote syslog messages.
facility.x-typeDescription: Facility to use when logging to Remote Syslog Server.

maxsize.description: Log size (in megabytes), which would trigger rotation.

period.description: The user configured period of logrotation.

target.description:
The user configured URI of the remote host.
If not specified log files will be rotated
locally.

vrf.description:
VRF for logrotate remote host connections.
If not set, then the 'default' VRF will be used.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

notification-threshold.description:
Threshold percentage relative to the size of the
log buffer. Once the utilization has reached this threshold, a
log notification will be generated.

audit-log.description: Configure the logging threshold for audit log buffer.

auth-log.description: Configure the logging threshold for auth log buffer.

commands-log.description: Configure the logging threshold for commands log buffer.

event-log.description: Configure the logging threshold for event log buffer.

https-server-log.description: Configure the logging threshold for http-server log buffer.

security-log.description: Configure the logging threshold for security log buffer.

severity.description:
Minimum severity for logging.
For AP devices, Range: EMERGENCY, ALERT, CRITICAL, ERROR, WARNING, NOTICE, INFO, DEBUG.
For PVOS devices, Range: MAJOR, INFO, WARNING, DEBUG, ERROR.
severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

ArubaLogging_LoggingSchemaGET.description:
Manage logging profiles for Aruba devices, enabling centralized control
of log message destinations, severity levels, and logging behavior.
This API allows administrators to define and retrieve logging profiles,
which can be referenced by other system or device profiles to standardize
log management. Use this API to retrieve the list of Logging profiles.

profile.description:
Defines a single Logging profile, specifying parameters for log message handling, destinations,
and severity filtering. Each entity is identified by name. Use these APIs to create, modify,
or delete an instance.

name.description:
Unique name for the Logging profile. Required to
reference this profile in device or system configurations.

origin-id.description: Origin Id for the syslog message.

x-enumDescriptions.HOSTNAME: Hostname of the device as the origin-id.
x-enumDescriptions.IP_ADDRESS: IP address of the outgoing interface as the origin-id.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Disable origin-id in the Syslog message.

origin-id.x-typeDescription: Specify the origin information for the syslog message.

prefix.description: Add a string to the message content. The string may contain upto 48 US ASCII printable characters except for space.

priority-description.description:
A text string associated with the values of facility, severity, and
system-module.

running-config-change.description:
Notification about changes in running configuration will
be sent to the syslog server(s).

transmission-interval.description: Egress rate limit for sending notifications.

reference this profile in device or system configurations.

module.description: List of module type, module and sub-module.

client.description: Client name to filter debug logs

column.description: Column name to filter debug logs

entities-severity.description: Minimum log severity to filter debug logs
entities-severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

instance.description: Instance entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

ip.description:
IPv4 or IPv6 entity and its corresponding value of this
sub-module.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

mac.description: MAC entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

port.description: Port entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

table.description: Table name to filter debug logs

ubt-zone.description: UBT Zone entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

vlan-id.description: VLAN entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.
vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

vrf.description: VRF entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

severity.description:
Specifies the minimum severity level for log messages to be sent to the configured destination.
Only messages at this level or higher will be logged.
severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

trace.description: trace value for debugging specific user or AP

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IEEE 802 MAC address

type-process-subcategory.description:
key for module. This key is the combination of type, process and subcategory separated by ~
type        - Logging type.
process     - Name of the debug process.
subcategory - Name of the debug subcategory type.

control-description.description: A text string associated with the given IP address.

disable.description: Disable sending syslog messages to the remote syslog server.

filter-name.description: Name of the filter used to filter syslog messages.

format.description: Log message format for the remote syslog server. Options include STANDARD (traditional syslog RFC 3164 format) or CEF (Common Event Format for SIEM integration with structured fields). Note: CEF format cannot be combined with TLS or source-interface options on Gateway devices.

host.description: Specifies the syslog server destination as a fully qualified domain name (FQDN), hostname, or IP address (IPv4/IPv6). Platform-Specific Behavior: AP Devices support IPv4 or IPv6 addresses with a maximum of 3 remote syslog servers per AP device. Gateway Devices require the host value to include both the IP address and logging category separated by '~' (e.g., 192.0.2.1~SECURITY). Supported categories: AP_DEBUG, NETWORK, SECURITY, SYSTEM, USER, USER_DEBUG, WIRELESS (case-sensitive). CX/PVOS Devices support standard FQDN, hostname, or IP formats without additional syntax requirements.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

host-filter.description: filter associated with the given IP address.

include-auditable-events.description:
Specifies whether auditable events (audit and authentication)
should be transmitted to the remote syslog server.

local-facility.description: Syslog facility code used to categorize log messages sent to the remote server. Facility codes (e.g., LOCAL0-LOCAL7) help syslog servers organize and filter incoming messages by source system or component. Default: LOCAL0.
local-facility.x-typeDescription: Facility to use when logging to Remote Syslog Server.

logging-syslog-enable.description: Enable this for non default vrf

mgmt.description: Syslog server for logs reachable through mgmt interface

port-number.description: Port number of the syslog server.
port-number.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

rate-limit.description: Rate limiting applied to messages sent to the remote syslog server.

burst.description: Maximum number of logs allowed in a given rate limit interval.

severity.description:
Syslog message severity. Only messages with severity higher than
or equal to the specified value will be sent to the remote server.
severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

smm.description:
syslog server should receive log messages from a standby management
module.

source-interface.description: Source address of outgoing Syslog messages, only support IPv4. Must match an existing VLAN interface ID.
source-interface.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tls.description: Enables TLS encryption for syslog traffic to the remote server. When enabled, log messages are transmitted over an encrypted TLS connection providing confidentiality and integrity. Default: false. Constraints: TLS is only supported for IPv4 remote server addresses (not IPv6) and cannot be combined with source-interface or CEF format options on Gateway devices.

tls-auth-mode.description: TLS authentication mode used to authenticate the server

x-enumDescriptions.SUBJECT_NAME: certificate and subject name based authentication.

tls-auth-mode.x-typeDescription: TLS authentication mode used to authenticate the server.

transport.description: Transport layer protocol used to forward messages to the server.
transport.x-typeDescription: Transport layer protocol used to forward messages to the server.

type.description: Logging category or type for filtering which log messages are sent to the remote syslog server. This allows selective forwarding of specific log categories such as security events, network events, or user activity to different syslog destinations.

unsecure-tls-renegotiation.description:
Enable TLS session with syslog server which does not support secure
renegotiation.

vrf.description:
VRF of the remote_host.
If not specified, remote-host will be routed through default VRF.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

module.description: List of module type, module and sub-module.

client.description: Client name to filter debug logs

column.description: Column name to filter debug logs

entities-severity.description: Minimum log severity to filter debug logs
entities-severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

instance.description: Instance entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

ip.description:
IPv4 or IPv6 entity and its corresponding value of this
sub-module.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

mac.description: MAC entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

port.description: Port entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

table.description: Table name to filter debug logs

ubt-zone.description: UBT Zone entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.

vlan-id.description: VLAN entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.
vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

vrf.description: VRF entity and its corresponding value of this sub-module.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

severity.description:
Specifies the minimum severity level for log messages to be sent to the configured destination.
Only messages at this level or higher will be logged.
severity.x-typeDescription: Logging level upto which, the messages are logged.

trace.description: trace value for debugging specific user or AP

oneOf.x-typeDescription: An IEEE 802 MAC address

type-process-subcategory.description:
key for module. This key is the combination of type, process and subcategory separated by ~
type        - Logging type.
process     - Name of the debug process.
subcategory - Name of the debug subcategory type.

control-description.description: A text string associated with the given IP address.

disable.description: Disable sending syslog messages to the remote syslog server.

filter-name.description: Name of the filter used to filter syslog messages.

format.description: Log message format for the remote syslog server. Options include STANDARD (traditional syslog RFC 3164 format) or CEF (Common Event Format for SIEM integration with structured fields). Note: CEF format cannot be combined with TLS or source-interface options on Gateway devices.

host.description: Specifies the syslog server destination as a fully qualified domain name (FQDN), hostname, or IP address (IPv4/IPv6). Platform-Specific Behavior: AP Devices support IPv4 or IPv6 addresses with a maximum of 3 remote syslog servers per AP device. Gateway Devices require the host value to include both the IP address and logging category separated by '~' (e.g., 192.0.2.1~SECURITY). Supported categories: AP_DEBUG, NETWORK, SECURITY, SYSTEM, USER, USER_DEBUG, WIRELESS (case-sensitive). CX/PVOS Devices support standard FQDN, hostname, or IP formats without additional syntax requirements.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:

zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

host-filter.description: filter associated with the given IP address.

include-auditable-events.description:

ArubaLoopProtect_LoopProtectSwitchSystem.description:
Loop protect configuration.Loop protection can find loops in
untagged layer 2 links as well as tagged VLAN.

mode.description: Sets whether loop-protect mode is either port based or vlan based.

x-enumDescriptions.PORT_MODE: Set loop protection to work on a port basis.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN_MODE: Set loop protection to work on a VLAN basis.

re-enable-timer.description: Time interval for enabling port blocked by loop-protect.

trap.description: Enable SNMP traps when a loop is detected by loop-protect.

items.description:
List of VLANs for sending loop-protect pdus(Protocol Data Unit)
for a port.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

accounting-group.description: Specifies the list of server groups and the order in which they will be applied for accounting of management sessions.

x-enumDescriptions.HTTPS_SERVER: https-server Session consists of both web and rest
x-enumDescriptions.SYSTEM: accounting for system reset, reboot and accounting on/off

acct-instances.description:
Server group configuration.
- For CX, the instance shall be a server group or local accounting.

mgmt-server-group.description: Management server group.null in case of accounting-method LOCAL

seq-id.description: The sequence id determines the accounting method order.

x-enumDescriptions.START_STOP: Send start and stop accounting record

acct-session.description: Configure how the accounting sessions are identified.

x-enumDescriptions.COMMON: Use a common ID for all accounting services.
x-enumDescriptions.UNIQUE: Use a Unique Session ID for each accounting services.

acct-switch-id.description: Include Switch identity when generating the session-id.

failthrough.description:
Enable accounting fail-through. This determines whether to access the next remote AAA server in case accounting is not recorded with the current server, the accounting will be attempted with the next configured server.
If true, the next server in line will be accessed.
If false, accounting will not be attempted with the next server.

supress-null-username.description: Supress accouting for users with no username

ArubaManagementUserGroup_MgmtUserGroup.description:
System management user groups configuration.
RBAC (Role Based Access Control) allows a user to create user-defined groups with configurable rules.
Users trying to execute operations on the switch will be restricted based on the rules configured under the user-defined group.
In other words, RBAC allows a network administrator to configure local authorization.

description.description: Description for the management user group.

inherit-group.description:
Inherit RBAC rules from another user group.
Only one user-group can be inherited. If needed, cascading/hierarchical
inheritance of rules can be achieved. RBAC rules will be `prepended`
to the rules configured for the current user group.

name.description:
Management user group name. For CX, up to 32 local user-groups are supported, 3 of which are built-in groups and their names cannot be reused,
which are administrators, operators and auditors.

seq-number.description:
Authorization REST RBAC rule sequence number.
Rules will be applied in the ascending order of sequence numbers.

rule.description: User group RBAC rules, rbac-action and rbac-match-command must also be configured.

seq-number.description:
Authorization RBAC rule sequence number. The rules will be evaluated from the smallest sequence number first.
For CX, the sequence number is mandatory to add any rule.

log.description: Enable event log for the matched CLI command

ArubaManagementUserGroup_RbacRules.description: User group RBAC CLI rules configuration

description.description: Set text comment for authorization rule.

action.description: RBAC rule action for the matched CLI command. Command will be permitted/denied based on rule's action.

log.description: Enable event log for the matched CLI command

match-command.description:
The CLI command to be matched.
For CX, user has to provide first word of the command followed by an optional POSIX regex for command arguments, all enclosed in double quotes (Use .* to match all commands)

ArubaManagementUser_ManagementUsersSchem.description:
Configure management users with role and authorization. This feature is applicable for all devices.
For AP, Users from higher-level configurations are automatically inherited and displayed at lower levels.
When a lower-level user has the same role name as a higher-level user, the lower-level user takes precedence.

user.description:
Management user parameter configuration.
For AP, Users from higher-level configurations are automatically inherited and displayed at lower levels.
When a lower-level user has the same role name as a higher-level user, the lower-level user takes precedence.

aging-period.description: Password aging time for a user.

authorized-key.description: Public keys for SSH authentication of a user

public-key.description: SSH public key of the user

ciphertext-password.description: Management user password in hash or ciphertext.

format.description:
Enable clear, cipher or hash text as the password input format.
For AP, Range: CLEARTEXT, CIPHERTEXT.

x-enumDescriptions.SHA1: User password in SHA1 hash format
x-enumDescriptions.SHA256: User password in SHA256 hash format

interface.description: The management interfaces through which the user can access the switch.

console.description: Allow console login for the user.

https-server.description: Allow REST and WebUI login for the user.

ssh.description: Allow SSH login for the user.

telnet.description: Allow Telnet login for the user.

max-concurrent-sessions.description: Maximum concurrent session for this management user. A value of 0 will unset it.

min-password-length.description: Minimum password length for a user.

name.description: Unique name for the given username on the device, which may either be the same as or different from the username.

old-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

password.description:
Management user password in plaintext.
- For a CX device, Maximum length is 64 characters.
If not configured, it indicates that the user is created with an empty password
password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

rolename.description: Configure user-role. For example, user with read-only privilege or the guest management user.

x-enumDescriptions.LOCAL: Indicate the type of management users with local privilege.

user-group.description:
Management user group name.
- For a CX device, user group is mandatory for creating a user.
Built-in user groups are as follows:
administrators: System-defined group with associated privilege level of 15.
               Members have access to:
                 All the CLI commands and bash shell.
                 All the REST Methods through REST APIs.
operators:      System-defined group with associated privilege level of 1.
               Members have access to
                 All show CLI commands (except those that display sensitive information).
                 All GET requests through REST APIs.
auditors:       System-defined group with associated privilege level of 19.
               Members have access to:
                 All show CLI commands to display and copy security logs.
                 All GET requests for security logs through REST APIs.
Only users belonging to the above three default groups will be allowed access to REST APIs.

username.description:
Username of the management user.
- For a CX device, Maximum length is 32 characters.
- For a PVOS device, valid length is 1..16.
- For AP, valid length is 1..32.

aging-period.description: Password aging time for a user.

authorized-key.description: Public keys for SSH authentication of a user

public-key.description: SSH public key of the user

ciphertext-password.description: Management user password in hash or ciphertext.

format.description:
Enable clear, cipher or hash text as the password input format.
For AP, Range: CLEARTEXT, CIPHERTEXT.

x-enumDescriptions.SHA1: User password in SHA1 hash format
x-enumDescriptions.SHA256: User password in SHA256 hash format

x-enumDescriptions.LOCAL: Indicate the type of management users with local privilege.

user-group.description:
Management user group name.
- For a CX device, user group is mandatory for creating a user.
Built-in user groups are as follows:
administrators: System-defined group with associated privilege level of 15.
               Members have access to:
                 All the CLI commands and bash shell.
                 All the REST Methods through REST APIs.
operators:      System-defined group with associated privilege level of 1.
               Members have access to
                 All show CLI commands (except those that display sensitive information).
                 All GET requests through REST APIs.
auditors:       System-defined group with associated privilege level of 19.
               Members have access to:
                 All show CLI commands to display and copy security logs.
                 All GET requests for security logs through REST APIs.
Only users belonging to the above three default groups will be allowed access to REST APIs.

username.description:
Username of the management user.
- For a CX device, Maximum length is 32 characters.
- For a PVOS device, valid length is 1..16.
- For AP, valid length is 1..32.

ArubaNaeAgent_NaeAgentsSchemaGET.description:
Configure and Manage Network Analytics Engine (NAE) agents.
Network Analytics Engine (NAE) is a troubleshooting solution that monitors switch database resources
and executes operations when monitor conditions are met.
NAE Agent is a running instance of an NAE script that acts as a template for agent creation.
When a new agent is created, all monitors specified in the script become active and alerts are
generated when conditions are met. Each agent:
- References an existing NAE script as its template
- Can have custom parameter values specific to this agent instance
- Monitors data every 5 seconds and generates alerts when conditions are satisfied
- Can be temporarily disabled to stop monitoring without deleting the configuration
- Operates independently, allowing multiple agents from the same script with different parameters
Agents provide real-time network analytics and automated troubleshooting capabilities.

agent-name.description: An agent name consisting of alphanumeric and underscore characters.

ciphertext-value.description: The ciphertext value of the agent parameter.

name.description: Name of an agent parameter as listed in the script's ParameterDefinitions.

value.description: The plaintext value of the agent parameter.

script-name.description: Name of an existing script run by the agent.

Tags for the script.

ArubaNaeScript_NaeScriptsSchemaGET.description:
Configure and Manage Network Analytics Engine (NAE) Scripts.
NAE Script is a Python-based monitoring script that defines resources to monitor (Monitors) via REST API
and conditions on those resources (Conditions). When conditions are met, pre-defined actions such as
syslog messages or CLI commands are triggered.
The main components of an NAE Script are:
- Manifest: Identifies the script with metadata (name, version, author, supported platforms, etc.)
- ParameterDefinitions: Defines customizable parameters for agent creation
- Agent constructor: Contains the monitoring and condition logic
Each script serves as a template for creating one or more NAE agents. Once uploaded and validated,
the script can be used to instantiate multiple agents with different parameter values.

name.description: A script name consisting of alphanumeric and underscore characters.

plaintext-script.description: The NAE script contents in plaintext format.

ArubaNtp_NtpSchemaGET.description:
Network Time Protocol (NTP) synchronizes system clocks across network
devices to ensure consistent timestamp accuracy for logs,
authentication protocols, and distributed operations. This module
supports NTPv3/v4 with IETF RFC 5905, configurable
authentication via MD5/SHA keys, and flexible server management.
Configure NTP servers, authentication profiles, conductor
associations, and source interfaces for time synchronization. Device
Limits: Gateway supports up to 15 NTP servers per profile. Use this
API to retrieve the list of NTP profiles.

profile.description:
NTP profile defining time server associations, authentication
parameters, and synchronization settings. Each entity is
identified by profile name. Use these APIs to create,
modify, or delete an NTP profile instance.

name.description:
Profile name, upon creation and scope mapping,
NTP functionality will be automatically enabled
on the device.

vrf.description: Client VRF used to request time, ensure the VRF is pre-configured.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

ArubaNtp_ServerConfig.description: Grouping for the NTP server configurations.

operation-mode.description: NTP operation mode, either unicast or broadcast (default).

server-mode-disable.description:
NTP server mode, sets default access control for client to
interact with server.

address.description:
Supported server entries are FQDN,
IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

burst.description:
Packet transmission mode when server(s) are reachable,
only valid when the transmission mode is set to BURST.

x-enumDescriptions.ALWAYS:
Sends a burst of packets to the server
to synchronize time.
x-enumDescriptions.INITIAL:
Sends a burst of packets initially to
the server to synchronize time.
x-enumDescriptions.SINGLE:
Sends a single packet to the server
to synchronize time.

iburst.description:
Packet transmission mode when server(s) are unreachable,
only valid when the transmission mode is set to iBURST.

x-enumDescriptions.ALWAYS:
Sends a burst of packets to the server
to synchronize time.
x-enumDescriptions.INITIAL:
Sends a burst of packets initially to
the server to synchronize time.
x-enumDescriptions.SINGLE:
Sends a single packet to the server
to synchronize time.

key-identifier.description:
Link authentication key identifier to server entry.
Ensure corresponding authentication key entry with identifier
exist.

mgmt-interface.description: Management interface used by client to connect to the server.

min-poll.description:
Minimum poll interval in 2^n seconds, must be less than maximum
poll interval.

prefer.description: The primary server preference for the client association.

tx-mode.description: Packet transmission mode to select the burst functionality.

x-enumDescriptions.BURST: Transmission burst mode when the server(s) are reachable.
x-enumDescriptions.IBURST: Transmission burst mode when the server(s) are unreachable.

version.description: Protocol version used by the client.

Configure packet capture profiles for network troubleshooting and analysis.
Packet capture allows monitoring and recording network traffic on datapath
and controlpath for diagnostic purposes.

filters, and parameters for network traffic monitoring.

or UDP.

ArubaRemoteManagement_RemoteManagementSc.description:
Remote management enables centralized configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting
of Aruba devices from external management platforms. This feature allows administrators
to securely connect to and control devices remotely, streamlining operations and support.
Use this API to retrieve the list of remote management profiles.

profile.description:
A remote management profile defines the parameters for connecting Aruba devices
to external management systems, including authentication, connection settings,
and access controls. Each profile is identified by its unique name. Use these APIs
to create, modify, or delete an instance.

activate-force-provision.description:

provision service

Central alternative location for CoP High Availability. Accepts FQDN or IP address
(E. g: 'device-nestle-d2.arubathena.com' or 'https://127.0.0.1').
For IPv6 addresses, enclose the address in square brackets.
(E. g: '[2620:11d:60a3:91::1030]' or '[2000::2]')

when using the alternative location (E. g: 'default')
