profile.description: RMON alarm parameters.

description.description:
The RMON alarm table periodically takes statistical samples from the
SNMP MIB objects in the probe and compares them to previously
configured thresholds. A hysteresis mechanism is implemented to limit
the generation of alarm. This mechanism generates one event as a
threshold is crossed in the appropriate direction for the SNMP MIB
object. No more events are generated for that threshold until the
opposite threshold is reached.

rmon.description: RMON alarm parameters for given index.

falling-threshold.description:
Falling threshold to be compared against the current value
to trigger the SNMP alarm.

index.description: An index that uniquely identifies the RMON alarm.

interval.description:
The interval in seconds, over which the snmp-oid is sampled
and compared with the rising and falling thresholds.

rising-threshold.description:
Rising threshold to be compared against the current value
to trigger the SNMP alarm.

sample-type.description: The method of sampling of the variable.

x-enumDescriptions.ABSOLUTE:
Absolute value of the variable will be compared with the
thresholds.
x-enumDescriptions.DELTA:
The difference between the current and previous sample of
the variable will be compared with the thresholds.

sample-type.x-typeDescription: The method of sampling of the variable.

snmp-oid.description:
The object identifier to be monitored.
Only variables that resolve to an ASN.1 primitive type of INTEGER
(INTEGER, Integer32,Counter32, Counter64, Gauge32, or TimeTicks)
will be monitored.

thresholds.
x-enumDescriptions.DELTA:
The difference between the current and previous sample of
the variable will be compared with the thresholds.

sample-type.x-typeDescription: The method of sampling of the variable.

snmp-oid.description:
The object identifier to be monitored.
Only variables that resolve to an ASN.1 primitive type of INTEGER
(INTEGER, Integer32,Counter32, Counter64, Gauge32, or TimeTicks)
will be monitored.

ArubaSnmpTrap_SnmpTrapSchemaGET.description:
SNMP Trap profiles configuration.
Using this profile SNMP traps can be enabled/disabled.
Enabled traps will notify important events to the management station.
This profile allows specification of traps,alarms.

ArubaSnmp_SnmpSchemaGET.description:
Profile for SNMP configuration.
SNMP user-table, trap receivers, community strings,
notify groups, targets, etc. can be configured.
Both Informs and Traps are supported.
Handles configuration for SNMP v1, v2c, v3 versions.

profile.description: SNMP profile to configure SNMP parameters.

agent-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

access-level.description:
ro - read-only access for SNMP community.
rw - read-write access for SNMP community.

x-enumDescriptions.MANAGER: manager level access for snmp user.
x-enumDescriptions.OPERATOR: operator level access for snmp user.

policy-ipv4.description: IPv4 policy associated with this community string.

policy-ipv6.description: IPv6 policy associated with this community string.

community.description: SNMP community associated with this context.

vrf.description: The VRF associated with this context.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

enable-snmpv3-only.description: Enables only the SNMPv3 mode of operation and disallows SNMP v1 and v2c.

inform-queue-length.description: To set inform queue length. By default inform queue length set to 250.

x-enumDescriptions.BOTH: Enable SNMP Server on both OOBM and Data planes.
x-enumDescriptions.DATA: Enable SNMP Server on Data Plane only.
x-enumDescriptions.MANAGEMENT_INTERFACE: Enable SNMP Server on Management Interface only.

notification-receiver.description: List of SNMP Notification receivers (traps or informs).

host-engineid.description:
Engine ID of SNMP notification host.
This parameter is applicable for v3 trap host type only.
host-engineid.x-typeDescription: Engine ID of SNMP host in HEX eg. 8000052301A9FEA484.

host-port-version-receiver-name-vrf.description: Key combination of host,port,version and receiver-name separated by ~

inform.description:
Acknowledgement will be received from SNMP server for inform notification type.
If inform is not set, notifcation type is trap.
This parameter is applicable for v2c & v3 version host only.

interval.description: Inform retry interval duration in seconds.

management-interface.description: Enable management port usage for SNMP notification receiver.

items.description: The type of this notification

retrycount.description: Number of retries to this host before giving up on the Inform.

items.description: List of trap groups.

trap-level.description: Trap level used for logging events to notification receiver.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: All log events.
x-enumDescriptions.NOTINFO: Only critical, warn and debug level log events.

remote-user.description: List of users on a remote SNMP engine who receives the inform notifications.

auth-pass-text.description: Authentication and privacy-protocol password text format

auth-password.description: Auth protocol password for SNMP inform notifications.
auth-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

auth-protocol.description: Auth protocols supported for SNMP inform notifications.

engine-id.description: Engine id of the remote system for V3 informs.
engine-id.x-typeDescription: Engine ID of SNMP host in HEX eg. 8000052301A9FEA484.

engine-id-username.description: Key combination of engine-id and username separated by ~

privacy-password.description: Privacy protocol password for SNMP inform notifications.
privacy-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

privacy-protocol.description: Privacy protocols supported for SNMP inform notifications.

username.description: The user name that receives inform notifications on a remote device.

response-source.description: Source IP address of the SNMP response PDU.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

x-enumDescriptions.DEST_IP_ADDRESS:
The destination ip address of the SNMP request is used
as the source IP address in the SNMP response.
x-enumDescriptions.LOOPBACK:
For the specified loopback interface, lexicographically
minimum configured ip address will be used as the source
ip address in the snmp response pdu.

restricted-access.description: Enable SNMPv1 and SNMPv2c access properties.

vrf.description: The VRF associated with this trap-source.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

snmp-source-controller-ip.description: On enabling this parameter source address of SNMP responses will be set to Controller IP.

type.description:
INCLUDE - Include an OID tree.
EXCLUDE - Exclude an OID tree.

x-enumDescriptions.EXCLUDE: MIB subtree excluded in SNMP view.
x-enumDescriptions.INCLUDE: MIB subtree included in SNMP view.

view-name-oid-tree-mask.description: The key combination consists of the SNMP view name, OID tree, and mask, separated by '~'. If the mask is empty, use 'EMPTY' as its value.

items.description: The VRF on which SNMP server is enabled.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

vrrp-id.description:
VRRP instance ID. This enables to use VRRP IP address
as source address in SNMP traps.
Only VRRP-ID or IP address as SNMP trap-source can be configured and not both.

agent-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

access-level.description:
ro - read-only access for SNMP community.
rw - read-write access for SNMP community.

x-enumDescriptions.MANAGER: manager level access for snmp user.
x-enumDescriptions.OPERATOR: operator level access for snmp user.

policy-ipv4.description: IPv4 policy associated with this community string.

policy-ipv6.description: IPv6 policy associated with this community string.

community.description: SNMP community associated with this context.

vrf.description: The VRF associated with this context.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

enable-snmpv3-only.description: Enables only the SNMPv3 mode of operation and disallows SNMP v1 and v2c.

inform-queue-length.description: To set inform queue length. By default inform queue length set to 250.

x-enumDescriptions.BOTH: Enable SNMP Server on both OOBM and Data planes.
x-enumDescriptions.DATA: Enable SNMP Server on Data Plane only.
x-enumDescriptions.MANAGEMENT_INTERFACE: Enable SNMP Server on Management Interface only.

host-engineid.x-typeDescription: Engine ID of SNMP host in HEX eg. 8000052301A9FEA484.

host-port-version-receiver-name-vrf.description: Key combination of host,port,version and receiver-name separated by ~

inform.description:
Acknowledgement will be received from SNMP server for inform notification type.
If inform is not set, notifcation type is trap.
This parameter is applicable for v2c & v3 version host only.

interval.description: Inform retry interval duration in seconds.

management-interface.description: Enable management port usage for SNMP notification receiver.

items.description: The type of this notification

retrycount.description: Number of retries to this host before giving up on the Inform.

items.description: List of trap groups.

trap-level.description: Trap level used for logging events to notification receiver.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: All log events.
x-enumDescriptions.NOTINFO: Only critical, warn and debug level log events.

remote-user.description: List of users on a remote SNMP engine who receives the inform notifications.

ArubaSpeedTest_SpeedTestSchemaGET.description:
Speed test profiles enable Aruba devices to measure network throughput,
latency, and performance between endpoints. This feature helps administrators
diagnose connectivity issues, validate service levels, and optimize network performance.
Use this API to retrieve the list of speed test profiles.

profile.description:
A speed test profile defines the parameters for conducting network performance tests,
including endpoints, test duration, and measurement intervals. Each profile is identified
by its unique name. Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete an instance.

include-reverse.description:
The direction of traffic is reversed and sent from the server to the client.
This option enables Iperf to run the speed test for an extended duration.

omit.description: Number of initial seconds to omit.

on-boot.description: Run speed test on boot up.

x-enumDescriptions.TCP: Execute speed test profile using the TCP protocol
x-enumDescriptions.UDP: Execute speed test profile using the UDP protocol

protocol.x-typeDescription: Protocol on which to execute the speed test.

server-ip.description: IP address of the Iperf server which is used to run the speed test.
server-ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

server-port.description: Server port that the client needs to connect to execute the speed test.
server-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

time-interval.description:
Time interval(in seconds) to run the speed test on a regular basis.
The minimum time interval is 60 seconds.

window.description: TCP window size or socket buffer size sent to the server while running speed test.

include-reverse.description:
The direction of traffic is reversed and sent from the server to the client.
This option enables Iperf to run the speed test for an extended duration.

omit.description: Number of initial seconds to omit.

on-boot.description: Run speed test on boot up.

x-enumDescriptions.UDP: Execute speed test profile using the UDP protocol

server-ip.x-typeDescription:

zone.

server-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

ArubaSshServer_SshServerGlobalConfig.description: Grouping for SSH Server global configuration.

allow-list.description:
SSH host allow list configuration.
For CX device, maximum of 20 entries in allow-list is supported.

items.description: List of ipv4 hosts that are allowed to connect to the server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
The ipv4-address type represents an IPv4 address in
dotted-quad notation.  The IPv4 address may include a zone
index, separated by a % sign.
The zone index is used to disambiguate identical address
values.  For link-local addresses, the zone index will
typically be the interface index number or the name of an
interface.  If the zone index is not present, the default
zone of the device will be used.
The canonical format for the zone index is the numerical
format

The ipv4-prefix type represents an IPv4 address prefix.
The prefix length is given by the number following the
slash character and must be less than or equal to 32.
A prefix length value of n corresponds to an IP address
mask that has n contiguous 1-bits from the most
significant bit (MSB) and all other bits set to 0.
The canonical format of an IPv4 prefix has all bits of
the IPv4 address set to zero that are not part of the
IPv4 prefix.

items.description: List of ipv6 hosts that are allowed to connect to the server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
The ipv6-address type represents an IPv6 address in full,
mixed, shortened, and shortened-mixed notation.  The IPv6
address may include a zone index, separated by a % sign.
The zone index is used to disambiguate identical address
values.  For link-local addresses, the zone index will
typically be the interface index number or the name of an
interface.  If the zone index is not present, the default
zone of the device will be used.
The canonical format of IPv6 addresses uses the textual
representation defined in Section 4 of RFC 5952.  The
canonical format for the zone index is the numerical
format as described in Section 11.2 of RFC 4007.

The ipv6-prefix type represents an IPv6 address prefix.
The prefix length is given by the number following the
slash character and must be less than or equal to 128.
A prefix length value of n corresponds to an IP address
mask that has n contiguous 1-bits from the most
significant bit (MSB) and all other bits set to 0.
The IPv6 address should have all bits that do not belong
to the prefix set to zero.
The canonical format of an IPv6 prefix has all bits of
the IPv6 address set to zero that are not part of the
IPv6 prefix.  Furthermore, the IPv6 address is represented
as defined in Section 4 of RFC 5952.

certificate-as-authorized-key.description: Check authenticating certificate for login username instead of local authorized-keys.

algorithm.description:
SSH cipher type.
For CX device: rijndael-cbc@lystor.liu.se and 3des-cbc cipher types are not supported.

priority.description:
Priority of the SSH cipher.
For CX device: valid range: 1-9.

items.description: VRFs where SSH server is enabled.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

file-transfer.description: Enable the SSH file transfer server.

algorithm.description:
SSH host key algorithm.
For CX device: x509v3-ssh-rsa,  x509v3-sign-rsa,  x509v3-sign-rsa2, and ssh-dss
host-key algorithm types are not supported.

x-enumDescriptions.ECDSA_SHA2_NISTP384: SSH host key algorithm type ecdsa-sha2-nistp384.

x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_SSH_RSA: SSH host key algorithm type x509v3-ssh-rsa.

Priority of the SSH host key algorithm.
For CX device: valid range: 1-7.

x-enumDescriptions.DIFFIE_HELLMAN_GROUP16_SHA512: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type diffie-hellman-group16-sha512.
x-enumDescriptions.DIFFIE_HELLMAN_GROUP18_SHA512: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type diffie-hellman-group18-sha512.
x-enumDescriptions.DIFFIE_HELLMAN_GROUP_EXCHANGE_SHA1: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1.
x-enumDescriptions.DIFFIE_HELLMAN_GROUP_EXCHANGE_SHA256: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256.
x-enumDescriptions.ECDH_SHA2_NISTP256: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type ecdh-sha2-nistp256.
x-enumDescriptions.ECDH_SHA2_NISTP384: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type ecdh-sha2-nistp384.
x-enumDescriptions.ECDH_SHA2_NISTP521: SSH key exchange (kex) algorithm type ecdh-sha2-nistp521.

priority.description: Priority of the SSH key exchange algorithm.

x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_ECDSA_SHA2_NISTP256: SSH public key algorithm type x509v3-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256.
x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_ECDSA_SHA2_NISTP384: SSH public key algorithm type x509v3-ecdsa-sha2-nistp384.
x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_ECDSA_SHA2_NISTP521: SSH public key algorithm type x509v3-ecdsa-sha2-nistp521.
x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_RSA2048_SHA256: SSH public key algorithm type x509v3-rsa2048-sha256.
x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_SIGN_RSA: SSH public key algorithm type x509v3-sign-rsa.
x-enumDescriptions.X509V3_SSH_RSA: SSH public key algorithm type x509v3-ssh-rsa.

priority.description: Priority of the SSH public key algorithm.

rekey-time.description: Time in minutes before an SSH rekey is initiated.

rekey-volume.description: Amount of traffic in KB before an SSH rekey is initiated.

x-enumDescriptions.PASSWORD: Require a certificate and a password for two factor authentication.
x-enumDescriptions.RADIUS: Request authorization from a RADIUS server for two factor authentication.

ArubaSwitchProfiles_SwitchProfilesSchema.description:
Configure and Manage Switch Profiles.
System profiles set the overall capabilities and capacities of the switch, based on the selected profile used at boot time.
System profiles set capacities such as that of the hardware forwarding table.

System profiles provide you with the flexibility to configure switches based on their location in the network (for example, core, spine, leaf).
When a switch boots without a profile specifically configured, it boots with the default profile.
When a switch is configured with a non-default profile, the switch requires a reboot for the profile to be applied.

icmp.description:
ICMP Configurations.
The Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) is used
by network devices, including routers, to send error
messages and operational information indicating,
for example, that a requested service is not available
or that a host or router could not be reached.

burst-normal.description: Maximum number of ICMP replies to send per second.

echo-reply.description:
Enables echo replies to broadcast
echo requests.

ipv6-icmp-bucket-size.description:
Bucket-size to send
ICMP error message.

ipv6-icmp-error-interval.description:
Interval at which to send
IPv6 ICMP error messages.

redirect.description: Enable sending ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages.

throttle.description:
ICMP throttle (in seconds) is used to configure the packet
interval to send ICMPv4/v6 error and redirect messages.

unreachable.description: Enable sending ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages.

tcp.description:
TCP Configurations.
In TCP 3-way handshake, the switch may respond back with
SYN-ACK which might never reach the intended destination.
These unestablished connections queue up, they can prevent
new, legitimate requests from being handled. Hence there
is a need to handle these half-open requests with a timeout
threshold which can be user tunable.

syn-ack-retry.description:
Number of times TCP SYN-ACK packets are
resent in response to a TCP SYN request.

profile.description: List containing system profile attributes.

dot1x-auth.description: Container for 802.1X authentication attributes, used to enable or disable 802.1X features.

mac-auth.description: Container for MAC authentication attributes, used to enable or disable MAC authentication.

user-role.description: Container for aaa authorization user-role global attributes.

web-auth.description: Container for Web authentication attributes, used to enable or disable Web based authentication.

client-insight.description:
Configuration of Client-Insight.
This feature aims at capturing L2, L3 and L4 stage on-boarding detail of the
clients. The details published by client insight feature are consumed by
HPE Aruba Networking (HPE ANW) Central to provide better
insights into client activities.

contact.description: Device contact information, often an email.

default-device-type-associations.description: List of supported device types configuration for switch system

device-profile-ref.description: Associate a profile with this device-type.

supported-device-type.description: Specify an approved device-type in order to configure and attach a profile to it.
supported-device-type.x-typeDescription: Default Device types for device profile association

description.description: Description of the user defined system profile.

dfp-cppm-timer.description: Configures the timer for AOS-S switch to send the client data to ClearPass.

disable-factory-reset.description:
Prevent the manual hard reset to factory default by pressing down
and holding the reset button.

dynamic-arp-inspection.description:
Configuration of Dynamic ARP Inspection.
Dynamic ARP Inspection is defense mechanism can be put in place on a switch to protect
against various possible attacks using ARP. This can limit the amount of ARP activity
allowed from a host or on a port and can also ensure all ARP packets are consistent
with one or more binding database which can be created via various means.
It can also enforce integrity checks on the ARP packets to check against using
different MAC or IP in the Ethernet or IP header and the ARP header.

validate-destination-mac.description: Validate destination MAC address in ARP request

validate-ip.description: Validate source IP address in ARP request

validate-source-mac.description: Validate source MAC address in ARP request

enable-unsupported-transceiver.description: Allow or Disallow transceivers that are recognized as unsupported to be operational.

fastboot.description:
Enable/Disable fastboot for the system. When fastboot is enabled,
Most of the test under POST will be skipped. POST (Power On Self Test)
verifies the hardware functionality of various modules (subsystem/
line modules/ fabric module / interfaces) during boot-up.

internal-vlan-range.description: Specifies the starting and ending VLAN number for the range.

loop-protect.description: Loop protect profile to be applied.

mac-address-delimiter.description: The character used to separate the digits of the MAC address.

mac-age-time.description: MAC age time or how long the MAC address remains in the MAC table.

management-vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

max-vlan.description: Configure the maximum number of VLANs allowed on the device.

policy-in.description: The policy name for inbound (Ingress) traffic.

port-security.description: Container for the port security configurations.

primary-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

hw-resource-share-enable.description:
Enable hardware resource sharing for private VLAN secondary ports.
This configuration is applicable for certain AOS-CX switches only.
This configuration is available from 10.14 switch firmware version
and it is enabled by default. This configuration helps to get more
private VLAN secondary ports.

ethertype.description: The QinQ ethertype configuration. This is supported only in certain CX switches
ethertype.x-typeDescription: QinQ ether types either Ethertype 8100 or Ethertype 88A8.

tag-type.description: The QinQ tag type in hexadecimal, must have QinQ VLAN mode configuration.

vlan-mode.x-typeDescription: The QinQ (802.1Q-in-802.1Q) VLAN modes either S-VLAN or mixed VLAN mode.

trunk-dynamic-vlan-include.description:
Indicates if dynamically learned VLANs from MVRP and port-access should
be included or excluded on ports configured with vlan trunk allowed all.
By default, dynamic VLANs are not included in trunk allowed list.

unsupported-transceiver-logging.description: Enable or Disable unsupported transceiver logging.

unsupported-transceiver-logging-interval.description: Configure the logging interval for allowed unsupported transceivers feature.

virtual-mac.description:
Global virtual MAC configuration. An example usage would be EVPN
Symmetric IRB where EVPN advertises this MAC as the Router's
MAC for all the EVPN Symmetric Routes.

contact.description: Device contact information, often an email.

device-profile-ref.description: Associate a profile with this device-type.

supported-device-type.description: Specify an approved device-type in order to configure and attach a profile to it.
supported-device-type.x-typeDescription: Default Device types for device profile association

description.description: Description of the user defined system profile.

dfp-cppm-timer.description: Configures the timer for AOS-S switch to send the client data to ClearPass.

disable-factory-reset.description:
Prevent the manual hard reset to factory default by pressing down
and holding the reset button.

dynamic-arp-inspection.description:
Configuration of Dynamic ARP Inspection.
Dynamic ARP Inspection is defense mechanism can be put in place on a switch to protect
against various possible attacks using ARP. This can limit the amount of ARP activity
allowed from a host or on a port and can also ensure all ARP packets are consistent
with one or more binding database which can be created via various means.
It can also enforce integrity checks on the ARP packets to check against using
different MAC or IP in the Ethernet or IP header and the ARP header.

validate-destination-mac.description: Validate destination MAC address in ARP request

validate-ip.description: Validate source IP address in ARP request

validate-source-mac.description: Validate source MAC address in ARP request

enable-unsupported-transceiver.description: Allow or Disallow transceivers that are recognized as unsupported to be operational.

fastboot.description:
Enable/Disable fastboot for the system. When fastboot is enabled,
Most of the test under POST will be skipped. POST (Power On Self Test)
verifies the hardware functionality of various modules (subsystem/
line modules/ fabric module / interfaces) during boot-up.

internal-vlan-range.description: Specifies the starting and ending VLAN number for the range.

loop-protect.description: Loop protect profile to be applied.

mac-address-delimiter.description: The character used to separate the digits of the MAC address.

mac-age-time.description: MAC age time or how long the MAC address remains in the MAC table.

management-vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

max-vlan.description: Configure the maximum number of VLANs allowed on the device.

policy-in.description: The policy name for inbound (Ingress) traffic.

port-security.description: Container for the port security configurations.

primary-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

hw-resource-share-enable.description:
Enable hardware resource sharing for private VLAN secondary ports.
This configuration is applicable for certain AOS-CX switches only.
This configuration is available from 10.14 switch firmware version
and it is enabled by default. This configuration helps to get more
private VLAN secondary ports.

ethertype.description: The QinQ ethertype configuration. This is supported only in certain CX switches
ethertype.x-typeDescription: QinQ ether types either Ethertype 8100 or Ethertype 88A8.

tag-type.description: The QinQ tag type in hexadecimal, must have QinQ VLAN mode configuration.

vlan-mode.x-typeDescription: The QinQ (802.1Q-in-802.1Q) VLAN modes either S-VLAN or mixed VLAN mode.

trunk-dynamic-vlan-include.description:
Indicates if dynamically learned VLANs from MVRP and port-access should
be included or excluded on ports configured with vlan trunk allowed all.
By default, dynamic VLANs are not included in trunk allowed list.

unsupported-transceiver-logging.description: Enable or Disable unsupported transceiver logging.

unsupported-transceiver-logging-interval.description: Configure the logging interval for allowed unsupported transceivers feature.

virtual-mac.description:
Global virtual MAC configuration. An example usage would be EVPN
Symmetric IRB where EVPN advertises this MAC as the Router's
MAC for all the EVPN Symmetric Routes.

dot1x-auth.description: Container for 802.1X authentication attributes, used to enable or disable 802.1X features.

mac-auth.description: Container for MAC authentication attributes, used to enable or disable MAC authentication.

user-role.description: Container for aaa authorization user-role global attributes.

web-auth.description: Container for Web authentication attributes, used to enable or disable Web based authentication.

client-insight.description:
Configuration of Client-Insight.
This feature aims at capturing L2, L3 and L4 stage on-boarding detail of the
clients. The details published by client insight feature are consumed by
HPE Aruba Networking (HPE ANW) Central to provide better
insights into client activities.

ArubaSwitchSystem_UserRole.description: Grouping for aaa authorization user-role global attributes configurations

download.description: Enables the downloadable user role feature.

initial-role.description: Configure the default role used when no other role applies.

ArubaSwitchSystem_UserRoleGet.description: Grouping for aaa authorization user-role global attributes configurations

download.description: Enables the downloadable user role feature.

initial-role.description: Configure the default role used when no other role applies.

ArubaSysmon_SysmonSchemaGET.description:
The System Resources Monitor daemon collects system parameters
(i.e., CPU usage, memory usage, open FD’s) for the overall system
and all running daemons, then writes it into the OVSDB during every
poll interval (~10 sec is default and minimum). This captures the
current value of these monitoring parameters, which is then displayed
using CLI show commands.

profile.description: System monitor configuration profile.

polling.description: Enable the system monitor data polling.
Enable the system monitor data polling.
Enable the system monitor data polling.

ArubaSystemInfo_SystemInfoSchema.description:
System information provides key details about the Aruba device, including hardware model,
software version, serial number, and operational status. This information is essential for
inventory management, support, and troubleshooting. Use this API to retrieve system information
for Aruba devices.

hostname.description: The hostname assigned to the device for identification in network management systems and CLI prompts. Must be unique per device. All device types support alphanumeric characters (0-9, a-z, A-Z) and dash (-). For CX switches, maximum length is 32 characters; other device types support up to 128 characters with no additional restrictions for APs. Cannot be configured when hostname-alias is set.

hostname-alias.description: References an existing alias named 'sys_host_name' from the Aruba Alias module for device-specific hostname assignment. This parameter allows variable substitution across devices when the hostname parameter is not directly configured. Valid only when hostname is not set.

snmpv3-local-engine-id.description: The SNMPv3 engine identifier uniquely identifying this device to SNMP management systems. Must be unique across all communicating SNMPv3 devices in the network to prevent authentication conflicts. For CX switches, must be in colon-separated hexadecimal notation with 8-38 characters total. For Gateway devices, must be 10-24 hexadecimal digits without separators. Supported on Gateway, CX, and PVOS devices.
snmpv3-local-engine-id.x-typeDescription: Engine ID of SNMP host in HEX eg. 8000052301A9FEA484.

snmpv3-user-engine-id.description: User-defined SNMPv3 engine identifier for the system. Must be unique across all communicating SNMPv3 devices. Supported on PVOS switches only.
snmpv3-user-engine-id.x-typeDescription: Engine ID of SNMP host in HEX eg. 8000052301A9FEA484.

sys-description.description: A descriptive text about the device and/or device collection for display in SNMP system information. Used by network management tools for device categorization and identification. Supported on CX switches only. Corresponds to the 'snmp-server system-description' CLI command.

ArubaTelemetry_TelemetrySchemaGET.description:
Enables inline monitoring statistics for AP. The information is collected and forwarded to
AirWave to debug client connectivity issues. This feature is only applicable for AP.

profile.description:
A telemetry profile defines the parameters for collecting and exporting device data, including
destination servers, data types, and reporting intervals. Each profile is identified by
its unique name. Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete an instance.

auth-stats.description: Enables the client authentication statistics on the AP

dhcp-stats.description: Enables the DHCP statistics on the AP

dns-stats.description: Enables the DNS statistics on the AP

sta-stats.description: Enables the station passive monitor statistics on the AP

auth-stats.description: Enables the client authentication statistics on the AP

dhcp-stats.description: Enables the DHCP statistics on the AP

dns-stats.description: Enables the DNS statistics on the AP

sta-stats.description: Enables the station passive monitor statistics on the AP

auth-stats.description: Enables the client authentication statistics on the AP

dhcp-stats.description: Enables the DHCP statistics on the AP

dns-stats.description: Enables the DNS statistics on the AP

sta-stats.description: Enables the station passive monitor statistics on the AP

ArubaTimerange_TimeRangesSchemaGET.description:
Configure time range profiles on device to enable or diable access to an SSID during a specific period of time.
This feature is only applicable for AP.

time-range.description: Configure an absolute or periodic time profile.

end-date-time.description: End date in the mm/dd/yyyy format and end time in the hh:mm format.
end-date-time.x-typeDescription:
A date and time, expressed in the format described in RFC3339.
That is to say:

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ+-hh:mm

where YYYY is the year, MM is the month expressed as a two-digit
month (zero padding if required), DD is the day of the month,
expressed as a two digit value. T is the literal character 'T',
HH is the hour of the day expressed as a two digit number, using
the 24-hour clock, MM is the minute of the hour expressed as a
two digit number. Z is the literal character 'Z', followed by a
timezone offset expressed in hours (hh) and minutes (mm), both
expressed as two digit numbers. The time offset is specified as
a positive or negative offset to UTC using the '+' or '-'
character preceding the offset.

Optionally, fractional seconds can be expressed after the minute
of the hour as a decimal number of unspecified precision
reflecting fractions of a second.

range-type.description:
Time Range absolute or periodici.
Range: ABSOLUTE, PERIODIC.

x-enumDescriptions.ABSOLUTE: The SSID is made available only during the specified date and time range.
x-enumDescriptions.PERIODIC: The availability of the SSID will be periodically changed based on the time range set.

end-day-of-week.description: Enter the end day for the time range profile.

end-time.description:
End time. 24-hour time, expressed as HH:MM
HH:00-23, MM:00-59.

period.description:
Period type.
Range: DAILY, WEEKEND, WEEKDAY, DAY_OF_WEEK.

start-day-of-week.description: Specify any day of the week from Monday to Sunday.

start-time.description:
Start time. 24-hour time, expressed as HH:MM
HH:00-23, MM:00-59.

start-date-time.description: Start date in the mm/dd/yyyy format and start time in the hh:mm format.
start-date-time.x-typeDescription:

webservers.description: Configurations related to authentication, access level and user session management for webservers hosted in the device

absolute-session-timeout.description: User's absolute WebUI session timeout <30-3600> (seconds). A value of 0 means no timeout

access-mode.description:
Web servers' REST API data management configuration. This configuration applies to all users,
regardless of their authorization role. Range: read-only, read-write.
This configuration is AOSCX specific

x-enumDescriptions.READ_ONLY: Enable read operations only on the webserver
x-enumDescriptions.READ_WRITE: Enable both read and write operation on the webserver

access-mode.x-typeDescription: Data access options allowed for all clients of the webserver

bypass-cp-landing-page.description: Enable bypass captive portal landing page

certificate-auth-username.description:
Certificate field used to read the username when authenticating an user.
Only valid when 'mgm-auth' value is set to 'CERTIFICATE'.
This configuration is AOSCX specific

x-enumDescriptions.COMMON_NAME: Read the username from the CN field of the certificate
x-enumDescriptions.USER_PRINCIPAL_NAME: Read the username from the UPN field of the certificate

certificate-auth-username.x-typeDescription: Certificate field options to search username when using certificate-based authentication

certificate-authorization.description:
Authorization source for client-role assignment.
Only valid when 'mgm-auth' value is set to 'CERTIFICATE'.
This configuration is AOSCX specific

x-enumDescriptions.RADIUS: Use Radius server as authorization source for the client role

certificate-authorization.x-typeDescription: Authorization source options for client role assignment when using certificate-based authentication

items.description: Enable cipher suites. GCM encryption based cipher suites are default

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: Allow ALL cipher suites

enable.description: Enable web user interface access to the system. On PVOS, default value is true and for GWs default value is false

items.description:
VRFs where the webserver is enabled to be accessed.
This configuration is AOSCX specific
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

exclude-http-security-headers.description: Exclude security headers from HTTP response

https-port-443.description: Enable WebUI access on HTTPS port (443)

idle-timeout.description: Web management session timeout in seconds

listen-port.description: Port type(s) used for web management. Default is BOTH

x-enumDescriptions.BOTH: Enable web management on the data ports and OOBM port
x-enumDescriptions.DATA: Enable web management on the data ports only
x-enumDescriptions.OOBM: Enable web management on the Out-of-band Management(OOBM) port only

listen-port.x-typeDescription: The port types used for web management

management-style.description: Web management user interface style. Default is IMPROVED

x-enumDescriptions.IMPROVED: Use the next-generation web management interface
x-enumDescriptions.TRADITIONAL: Use the legacy web management interface

management-style.x-typeDescription: Web management user interface style options

management-url.description: URL for the web interface help page

max-clients.description: Configure Web servers' maximum supported concurrent clients <25-320>

max-sessions-per-client.description:
Web servers' maximum supported concurrent sessions per client.
Range: 1-8. This configuration is AOSCX specific

items.description: Authentication modes enabled for users to request access to the webserver resources

x-enumDescriptions.CERTIFICATE: Use Client Certificate Authentication to Configure Management User's WebUI Access
x-enumDescriptions.USERNAME_PASSWORD: Use Username/Password Authentication to Configure Management User's WebUI Access

items.x-typeDescription: Authentication options for accessing the system webserver

session-timeout.description: Configure Webserver user's session timeout for inactivity

ssl-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

items.description: Configure SSL/TLS protocol. Default is TLSv1.2

x-enumDescriptions.ALL: Support TLSv1, TLSv1.1, TLSv1.2

support-url.description: URL for the web interface support page

via-client-cert-port.description:
Configure Port number used for VIA client-cert based profile download.
Valid range is <1025-65535>. Default value is 8085

Dynamic DNS (DDNS) using IETF RFC 2136 protocol automatically updates
DNS records when device IP addresses change through DHCP or uplink
modifications. DDNS uses TSIG (Transaction Signature) authentication
for secure zone updates to authoritative DNS servers. Configure DDNS
update intervals, server lists with authentication keys (Gateway),
and PTR record options (Access Point). Server IPs are IPv4 only. Use
this API to retrieve the list of DDNS profiles.

get.description:
Configure and manage hardware module profiles.
A hardware module profile allows certain device-specific configuration to be applied
to a standalone switch, a chassis line-module or a VSF stack member. It is a mechanism
intended to ease the process of configuring these devices with the same set of
configurations. Instead of manually configuring each switch, line-module or VSF
member one-by-one, the hardware module profile collects the common configuration
settings and allows them to be applied to a specific device or a collection of
devices.
To apply a hardware module profile to a VSF member or group of VSF members, the
hw-profile name here must be referenced from their respective 'hw-profile' endpoints
in the 'switch-stack' API.
See `aruba-switch-stack:stacks/stack/members/hw-profile`.
Applicable to CX 6200, 6300, 6300L switches.
To apply a hardware module profile to a specific line-module or group of line-modules,
the profile name here must be referenced from their respective 'hw-profile' endpoint
in the switch-chassis' API.
See `aruba-switch-chassis:switch-chassis/chassis/line-modules/hw-profile`
Applicable to CX 6400, 8400X switches.
To apply a hardware module profile to a standalone switch, including devices that
support VSF but are not configured in a stack, no reference from another API is
required.

parameters.description: The name of the profile for the hardware module.
parameters.name: name

get.description:
The job scheduler feature facilitates us to schedule a job to be
executed in the near future.  Currently, only non-interactive enable
and configuration commands are allowed to be configured as a job.
Schedules can be configured to be triggered periodically or at a specific
calendar date and time. They are categorized into periodic schedules,
calendar schedules, and one-shot schedules. Periodic schedules are based
on the fixed time interval between the two triggers.

Calendar schedules are based on the specified days of the week and days
of the month. Calendar schedules are therefore aware of the notion of
months, days, weekdays, hours, and minutes.

One-shot schedules are similar to the calendar schedule but they will
automatically disable themselves after one trigger.

This feature is useful to schedule a toggling port, check the system
health status, clear the statistics or some clean-up activities,
QoS policy changes, save the configurations, and other similar jobs.

While updating trigger-type other parameter must set to null, which are
not applicable.
Scheduling entry parameters.

post.summary: Create a new schedule by unique name

get.description:
Configure and Manage Network Analytics Engine (NAE) agents.
Network Analytics Engine (NAE) is a troubleshooting solution that monitors switch database resources
and executes operations when monitor conditions are met.
NAE Agent is a running instance of an NAE script that acts as a template for agent creation.
When a new agent is created, all monitors specified in the script become active and alerts are
generated when conditions are met. Each agent:
- References an existing NAE script as its template
- Can have custom parameter values specific to this agent instance
- Monitors data every 5 seconds and generates alerts when conditions are satisfied
- Can be temporarily disabled to stop monitoring without deleting the configuration
- Operates independently, allowing multiple agents from the same script with different parameters
Agents provide real-time network analytics and automated troubleshooting capabilities.

parameters.description: An agent name consisting of alphanumeric and underscore characters.
parameters.name: agent-name

parameters.name: agent-name

post.summary: Create NAE agent

get.description:
Configure and Manage Network Analytics Engine (NAE) Scripts.
NAE Script is a Python-based monitoring script that defines resources to monitor (Monitors) via REST API
and conditions on those resources (Conditions). When conditions are met, pre-defined actions such as
syslog messages or CLI commands are triggered.
The main components of an NAE Script are:
- Manifest: Identifies the script with metadata (name, version, author, supported platforms, etc.)
- ParameterDefinitions: Defines customizable parameters for agent creation
- Agent constructor: Contains the monitoring and condition logic
Each script serves as a template for creating one or more NAE agents. Once uploaded and validated,
the script can be used to instantiate multiple agents with different parameter values.

parameters.description: A script name consisting of alphanumeric and underscore characters.

get.description:
Configure and Manage Switch Profiles.
System profiles set the overall capabilities and capacities of the switch, based on the selected profile used at boot time.
System profiles set capacities such as that of the hardware forwarding table.

System profiles provide you with the flexibility to configure switches based on their location in the network (for example, core, spine, leaf).
When a switch boots without a profile specifically configured, it boots with the default profile.
When a switch is configured with a non-default profile, the switch requires a reboot for the profile to be applied.

get.description:
The System Resources Monitor daemon collects system parameters
(i.e., CPU usage, memory usage, open FD’s) for the overall system
and all running daemons, then writes it into the OVSDB during every
poll interval (~10 sec is default and minimum). This captures the
current value of these monitoring parameters, which is then displayed
using CLI show commands.
System monitor configuration profile.
